--- title: '蘇打綠：他們花了二十年才知道，自己的名字也要打官司' description: '2001 年政大金旋獎舞台上四個學生組起來、2003 年貢寮海祭被林暐哲發掘、唱完韋瓦第四部曲走遍北京柏林、2019 年被告上法庭、2023 年才在小巨蛋拿回自己的名字——蘇打綠走完二十年才學會：台灣 indie 樂團出道第一件事，是先把團名註冊起來。' date: 2026-06-09 category: 'Music' tags: ['蘇打綠', '魚丁糸', '吳青峰', '台灣音樂', '著作權', '韋瓦第計畫', '林暐哲'] subcategory: '獨立與搖滾' author: 'Taiwan.md Contributors' featured: false lastVerified: 2026-06-09 lastHumanReview: true readingTime: 28 image: '/article-images/music/sodagreen-six-members-2014.jpg' imageCredit: 'Solomon203' imageLicense: 'CC BY-SA 4.0' imageSource: 'https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:2014%E8%98%87%E6%89%93%E7%B6%A010%E5%91%A8%E5%B9%B4%E4%B8%96%E7%95%8C%E5%B7%A1%E5%9B%9E%E6%BC%94%E5%94%B1%E6%9C%83-%E7%A9%BA%E6%B0%A3%E4%B8%AD%E7%9A%84%E8%A6%96%E8%81%BD%E8%88%87%E5%B9%BB%E8%A6%BA-42.jpg' viewpointFormed: true --- # 蘇打綠：他們花了二十年才知道，自己的名字也要打官司 > **30 秒概覽：** 2001 年五月，政大金旋獎舞台上四個學生用一首〈窺〉拿下樂團組最佳人氣獎，那個團叫「蘇打綠」。二十二年後的小巨蛋，主唱吳青峰深吸一口氣喊出「我們是『蘇打綠』！」，是宣告一場打了四年的法律戰結束[^1]。中間有韋瓦第四部曲走過倫敦、北京、柏林、台東；有第 27 屆金曲獎《冬 未了》一口氣抱走五座[^2]；有 2017 自由廣場兩萬人合唱的最後安可[^3]；也有 2019 之後一連串「魚丁糸」分身、商標訴訟、刑事起訴、五連敗的林暐哲[^4]。這篇是這個樂團從學生社團走進法庭、從法庭走回小巨蛋的二十年。 2001 年 5 月，國立政治大學體育館。第十八屆政大金旋獎決賽舞台上，四個學生站在一起。主唱吳青峰，中文系大一；貝斯手謝馨儀，企管系；鼓手史俊威，社會學系；吉他手「新哥」是青峰的高中朋友[^5]。他們的名字叫「蘇打綠」，那天他們以一首〈窺〉拿下樂團組最佳人氣獎[^5]。 沒有人想到，二十二年後，當主唱在小巨蛋舞台上喊出「我們是『蘇打綠』！」這個句子的時候，他正在宣告一場長達四年的法律戰結束[^1]。 那是一個樂團從學生社團走到法庭、從法庭再走回小巨蛋的二十年弧線。 --- ## 「窺」：政大金旋獎那個樂團組第一名 第十八屆政大金旋獎是 2001 年那場。場地是政大體育館，主辦是政大課指組與學生社團聯合處理[^6]。金旋獎在台灣校園歌唱比賽史上有一個位置——它跟師大的師大盃、輔大的輔仁之星並列台灣三大校園音樂獎，從 1984 年辦到 2026 年，已經四十屆。早年是民歌風氣餘溫，2000 年前後轉成獨立樂團孵化器：陳綺貞、糯米團、自然捲、蘇打綠這幾組都是從這個比賽舞台被音樂圈聽見的[^6]。 四個第一次組樂團的學生，要參加比賽得有一首作品。吳青峰拿出他高三時寫的那首〈窺〉。這首歌的來歷比比賽本身還早：1999 年，師大附中校內的「天韻獎」創作組，吳青峰以〈窺〉拿下冠軍[^7]。當時他十七歲。 金旋獎決賽那天，他們唱了〈窺〉。樂團組最佳人氣獎被他們拿走[^5][^7]。 蘇打綠的「蘇打」這兩個字是鼓手史俊威提的——舒爽氣泡的清新感[^8]。「綠」則是吳青峰最喜歡的顏色。團名就這樣組起來，沒有任何深奧的典故。後來訪談裡他們也坦白說，就只是順口好記[^8]。 > 💡 **你知道嗎？** 比起許多刻意設計概念的樂團名，「蘇打綠」這三個字是兩個成員的喜好疊在一起——一個提味道，一個提顏色。團名沒有深意，反而讓它後來成為一個容易被各種解讀附加上去的容器。二十年後當這個名字進入商標法庭時，沒有人去爭論這三個字「原本是什麼意思」——爭論的是「誰先去登記了它」。 從 2001 年 5 月到 2003 年 3 月，將近兩年的時間，蘇打綠並不穩定。中間吉他手換了三位：彥廷、小為、宏儒[^5]。一個成員換來換去的學生樂團，這在校園音樂圈再常見不過——團員念書、實習、退伍、跨校的生活節奏拉扯著一個樂團能不能延續。 吳青峰自己的學歷軌跡：高雄高雄中學畢業後，2000 年以推甄方式進入國立政治大學中文系[^9]。政大記憶網——政治大學圖書館建構的校史 wiki——把這個推甄入學的紀錄留了下來。在校期間他不是只待在中文系一個地方，廣播電台、合唱團、唱片設計都摸過。蘇打綠的早期形象，跟「政大校園民歌」的脈絡不一樣，更接近於 90 年代末到 2000 年代初台灣大學校園裡那波 indie 樂團的湧現——廠牌不一定有，工作室不一定有，但每個校園都有一個樂團組，每個樂團組都在校園比賽舞台上摸索。 謝馨儀則是吳青峰在政大認識的第一批朋友。她念企管系，跟史俊威是政大游泳隊的學妹學長——這是一個校園社群層層連結的時代，樂團的成形路徑通常是同一群人從一個社團活動連到另一個社團活動然後組起來的，跟「音樂圈先有需求再去找人」的職業路徑完全不同[^5]。 2003 年 3 月，是蘇打綠歷史上重要的一個刻度。這個月，三個新成員陸續加入：何景揚（政大公共行政研究所，木吉他，後來成為團長，暱稱阿福）、劉家凱（政大心理系，電吉他）、龔鈺祺（北藝大音樂學研究所碩士班，鋼琴與中提琴，暱稱阿龔）[^5][^10]。原本的四人加上這三位，蘇打綠的六人陣容確立。 但這裡有一個小小的史料分歧。中文維基百科把何景揚加入時間記成 2003 年 3 月，但何景揚自己在後來 Yahoo 訪談裡曾說，是「2004 年生日當天被青峰丟訊息」才加入的[^10]。兩個版本差了一年，至今沒有一個權威說法把這個分歧合一。對一個成員的入團日期記憶會差這麼多，可能反映了一件事：在那個時期，「蘇打綠」是個流動的名稱而不是固定的編制，誰算正式團員、誰是來幫一場演出，邊界是模糊的。 > 📝 **策展人筆記**：六位團員散在中文系、企管系、社會學系、公共行政研究所、心理系、北藝大音樂學研究所——五個系所、兩所大學。這個學科多元的組合在後來的韋瓦第計畫裡會被放大：一個樂團敢從民謠跨到搖滾、跨到國樂、跨到古典磅礡，跟組成它的六個人本來就有人讀社會學、有人讀音樂學、有人讀公行、有人讀心理或許不無關係。「樂團」這兩個字常被想像成「一群音樂科系學生」，但蘇打綠從一開始就是反例。 --- ## 中文系、企管系、社會系、北藝大音樂所 把六位團員的學科背景攤開來看，會有一個有趣的圖： **吳青峰**（主唱、詞曲創作），1982 年 8 月 30 日生，高雄人，師大附中畢業，政大中文系，2004 年起赴英國 University of Birmingham 進修一年。從〈窺〉的高中天韻獎冠軍開始，他寫詞寫曲的密度遠高於一般搖滾樂團主唱[^9]。 **謝馨儀**（貝斯），1982 年 4 月 16 日生，政大企管系，蘇打綠創團四人之一，後來在許多訪談裡是被定位為「樂團的家政組長」——管行政、管時間、管溝通。 **史俊威**（鼓手），1979 年 8 月 26 日生，政大社會學系。Yamaha 把他列在台灣藝人頁裡，他是蘇打綠的鼓手裡少數有專業器材代言關係的成員[^11]。「蘇打」這兩個字是他提的。 **何景揚**（木吉他、團長），1982 年 4 月 4 日生，政大公共行政研究所。蘇打綠的團長身分後來由他擔任。 **劉家凱**（電吉他），1982 年 2 月 5 日生，政大心理系。 **龔鈺祺**（鋼琴、中提琴），1980 年 12 月 16 日生，北藝大音樂學研究所碩士班——蘇打綠裡唯一一位科班音樂學歷的成員。後來韋瓦第計畫《冬 未了》閉關三個月一人完成 70 人交響樂譜的，就是他[^12]。 把六個人的學科並排：中文、企管、社會、公行、心理、音樂學。三個系在管理與政策、兩個系在人文社會、一個在音樂。這個組合放到任何一個 indie 樂團去看都不算「典型」。 樂團組成的故事在這個層次上會被低估。台灣 indie 樂團的常見書寫框架是「一群熱愛音樂的學生組起來」——但蘇打綠這群人的起點是政大校園裡同一個時間段、彼此認識、有人提議去報名校園比賽。校園比賽是第一個動機，作品反而是後來的事。 這個次序差異看起來小，但會影響後來的整個故事。一個從校園比賽出發的樂團，跟一個從錄音 demo 出發的樂團，對音樂工業的依賴方式不同。前者習慣於「上台、被聽見」這個 loop；後者習慣於「進錄音室、跟製作人協作」這個 loop。蘇打綠後來被林暐哲接走進入主流的時候，他們已經把「上台、被聽見」這個動作練了兩三年——那是一個不在 demo 裡的本能。 --- ## 「就是這一組了」：貢寮海祭熱浪搖滾舞台 ![2008 年 3 月 12 日，蘇打綠在台北 The Wall 公館的 live 演出，主唱吳青峰與五位團員同台](/article-images/music/sodagreen-the-wall-2008.jpg) _2008 年 3 月 12 日，蘇打綠於台北 The Wall 公館 live。Photo: Wikipedia user Solomon203, CC BY-SA 3.0. [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sodagreen-20080312TheWall.jpg)。_ 2003 年 7 月，新北市貢寮區的福隆海水浴場。第四屆海洋音樂祭。 蘇打綠當時還是個沒有專輯的學生樂團。他們報名了海祭的「熱浪搖滾」舞台——這個舞台跟海祭主舞台不是同一個位階，熱浪搖滾屬於小舞台，給校園與獨立樂團演出用[^5]。那次海祭，他們是「印夏天」解散巡迴的其中一場：之前還跑過西門町、台南烏托邦、高雄 ATT 秘密基地、台中老諾、台北大學博覽會[^5]。一個學生樂團跑全台小場地的標準巡迴，沒有什麼特別之處。 但那天的台下有一個人，叫林暐哲。 林暐哲，當時已經是台灣音樂圈的資深製作人。早年是 Baboo 樂團主唱，1990 年代轉做幕後，做過陳珊妮、楊乃文、陳綺貞、蘇打綠的製作[^13]。他在貢寮海祭那個熱浪搖滾的小舞台底下，聽到了吳青峰的聲音。 後來他在今周刊的訪談裡用一句話描述當下的判斷： > 「就是這一組了。」[^13] 這個瞬間後來被反覆引述，但圍繞著它的細節常常被簡化。比較準確的脈絡是這樣：當時音樂圈裡聽過吳青峰 demo 的人，對那個嗓音的反應是兩極的。有人覺得「不男不女」，有人覺得獨特。林暐哲後來把這個爭議改寫成一個更帶溫度的詞——「忽男忽女」——寫進蘇打綠唱片文案[^14]。「忽」這個字，比「不」溫柔了好幾度。 林暐哲不是一次就完全接受那個聲音。他在後來的訪談裡曾經回憶，自己第一次聽到吳青峰的聲音時，心裡其實是有點反感的，但身體比心裡誠實——身體把那首歌聽完了，他才意識到自己已經被吸進去了。這段話原始來源在網路檢索上幾經輾轉，現存只在二手轉述中能找到完整版本——所以這篇文章把它當作背景脈絡而不是直接引語。 林暐哲決定簽下蘇打綠，把他們納入自己「林暐哲音樂社」這個獨立廠牌底下。這個決定的意義在當時並不顯眼，但它將決定接下來十多年蘇打綠跟主流市場的關係——以及二十年後，這個決定會以另一種形式回頭來叩問所有人。 2004 年 5 月 30 日，蘇打綠在政大舉辦的「School Rock」演唱會上首次正式發行音樂[^15]。同台的還有陳珊妮跟陳綺貞——這兩位是當時台灣 indie 女聲的代表，蘇打綠跟她們同台，等於是被正式介紹進入這個圈子。發行的是兩首曲目的單曲，由林暐哲音樂社出版，是蘇打綠創團以來第一張正式商業 CD。林暐哲音樂社後來把 5 月 30 日命名為「蘇打綠日」——這個日期一直延續到 2022 年林暐哲在 FB 上放棄商標的那一篇聲明，他選的也是 5 月 30 日[^16]。 2005 年 9 月 3 日，蘇打綠發行了首張同名專輯《蘇打綠》。林暐哲音樂社製作，林暐哲擔任製作人，11 首歌，53 分 40 秒[^17]。預購送的是一本《蘇打誌 sodazine 1》——蘇打綠早期一個獨特的傳統，每張專輯都會附一本團員自己編寫的小誌，裡面有歌詞、寫作背景、照片、隨手記。這個小誌系列後來變成樂迷收藏的一部分。 首張專輯入圍了第 17 屆金曲獎最佳樂團獎，沒得獎，但被金曲體系注意到了。 > 📝 **策展人筆記**：「林暐哲音樂社」這個獨立廠牌的存在，在當時的台灣 indie 音樂工業裡有意義。那個時期的獨立樂團，要在主流體系生存有幾條路：被五大唱片公司簽走、跟主流經紀公司合作、自己摸著做。「林暐哲音樂社」走的是第三條路的變體：一個有主流製作經驗的資深製作人開了個小廠牌，把他看中的樂團納進來。這條路在當時是一條相對前衛的路，但也埋下了一個問題：當製作人同時是廠牌老闆、合約相對人、商標權持有人的時候，藝人跟製作人之間的權力關係，比典型的「藝人 vs 公司」要更貼身、也更模糊。
_〈小情歌〉2006 年 10 月《小宇宙》專輯主打。吳青峰一人包辦詞曲，林暐哲音樂社出版，後來成為 2007 年第 18 屆金曲獎最佳作曲。_ --- ## 韋瓦第帶他們去倫敦、北京、柏林 ![2014 年「空氣中的視聽與幻覺」十週年世界巡迴小巨蛋場，舞台燈光下蘇打綠演出畫面](/article-images/music/sodagreen-vivaldi-tour-2014-07.jpg) _2014 年 7 月，「空氣中的視聽與幻覺」十週年世界巡迴台北小巨蛋三連場現場。Photo: Wikipedia user Solomon203, CC BY-SA 3.0. [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Sodagreen)。_ 2006 年 10 月 20 日，蘇打綠發行第二張錄音室專輯《小宇宙》[^18]。同名主打與〈小情歌〉是專輯裡的兩個記憶點。〈小情歌〉的詞曲都由吳青峰一手包辦，旋律的乾淨、歌詞的口語，把這首歌推到那個時代台灣大學生的 KTV 必點清單裡。 2007 年的第 18 屆金曲獎，蘇打綠以《小宇宙》拿下最佳樂團獎，〈小情歌〉拿下最佳作曲人獎（吳青峰）[^2]。從 2003 年貢寮海祭被林暐哲發掘到拿到第一座金曲獎最佳樂團，中間花了不到四年。 同年發行的第三張錄音室專輯《無與倫比的美麗》裡，有一首歌叫〈四季狂想〉。這首歌後來被認定是韋瓦第計畫的萌芽：歌詞與旋律已經帶有四季流轉的概念雛形，但還沒被放大成一個跨越好幾年的專輯系列[^19]。 2008 年第 19 屆金曲獎，蘇打綠以《無與倫比的美麗》再拿一次最佳樂團獎[^2]。連續兩屆抱回最佳樂團，意味著他們已經從「金曲圈注意到的新人」走到了「金曲圈確認過的樂團」這個位置。 接下來的事情就是韋瓦第計畫。 韋瓦第計畫——名字取自巴洛克作曲家安東尼奧・韋瓦第（Antonio Vivaldi）的《四季》小提琴協奏曲——是蘇打綠在中文流行音樂史上規格相對特別的一個野心。它包含了**四個季節、四個城市、四種情緒、四張專輯**[^20]。整個系列從 2009 年第一張開始，到 2015 年最後一張收尾，中間夾著兩張不屬於韋瓦第系列的專輯，總跨度六年半。 時間軸這樣排： **春・日光**——2009 年 5 月 8 日發行，蘇打綠第五張錄音室專輯，主題城市是台東，樂風為「民謠春光」[^21]。錄音團隊把大型錄音棚換成台東的自然環境，跟在地音樂氣息結合。這是韋瓦第計畫第一張，也是把整個系列概念定錨的那一張。 **夏／狂熱**——2009 年 9 月 11 日發行，第六張錄音室專輯，錄音地點是倫敦 Miloco Studios "The Pool"——一個 1970 年代由 Pink Floyd 的 Roger Waters 收購、後來經 Miloco 接管的傳奇錄音場地。蘇打綠在那裡錄製這張專輯的支出據報導接近一千萬台幣[^22]。樂風是搖滾與 Britpop。 **秋：故事**——2013 年 9 月 18 日發行，第九張錄音室專輯，主題城市是北京，樂風是「詩意國風」，編曲中大量使用古箏、二胡、嗩吶這類中國傳統樂器[^23]。從夏到秋之間隔了整整四年——中間蘇打綠出了一張不屬於韋瓦第的專輯《你在煩惱什麼》（2011 年 11 月 11 日，第八張），同時阿福（何景揚）服役、阿龔（龔鈺祺）也面臨兵役安排，秋冬計畫被迫往後挪[^5]。 **冬 未了**——2015 年 11 月 4 日發行，第十張錄音室專輯，主題城市是柏林，樂風是「莊嚴的古典」，與 German Pops Orchestra（德國流行樂團，60 人交響團）合作錄音[^24]。 四張專輯，四個城市，四個季節，四種樂風。一個獨立樂團這樣計畫一個跨六年半的概念系列，在台灣 indie 史上沒有第二個對照組。 flipermag 在 2016 年的一篇韋瓦第計畫總評裡用一句話收： > 「『春』在台東屬於溫暖的民謠，『夏』在倫敦屬於熱情的搖滾，『秋』在北京屬於憂傷的詩歌，『冬』在柏林屬於莊嚴的古典。」[^20] 《冬 未了》的製作細節值得特別停一段。蘇打綠裡唯一科班音樂學歷的阿龔——龔鈺祺——一個人閉關三個月，完成了一份 70 人交響樂譜[^12]。柏林錄音時，他們與 German Pops Orchestra 的 60 人交響團合作。台灣 indie 樂團跟海外職業交響團合作錄整張專輯，這在當時是非常少見的規格。 > 💡 **你知道嗎？** 韋瓦第計畫第二張《夏／狂熱》在倫敦 Miloco Studios 錄音，支出據報導接近一千萬台幣[^22]。一個獨立樂團敢花一千萬台幣到倫敦錄一張專輯，這個數字放在當時的台灣 indie 音樂工業裡幾乎是反常識的——大多數 indie 樂團一張專輯的錄音預算不到這個數字的十分之一。把韋瓦第計畫稱為「奢侈品」並不為過，但這個奢侈是有產出的：六年半之後，第 27 屆金曲獎一口氣把五座獎送給了《冬 未了》。 第 27 屆金曲獎 2016 年的結果，是韋瓦第計畫整個系列的官方加冕： - 最佳國語專輯：**《冬 未了》** - 最佳樂團：**蘇打綠** - 最佳專輯製作人：**林暐哲** - 最佳作詞人：**吳青峰**（〈他舉起右手點名〉） - 最佳編曲人：**蘇打綠 + 林暐哲**（〈痛快的哀艷〉）[^25] 一張專輯五座金曲獎，這個成績在台灣金曲獎的歷史裡是少數案例。從 2007 年的第 18 屆到 2016 年的第 27 屆，蘇打綠把「最佳樂團」抱了三座——一個樂團三度拿下金曲最佳樂團，這意味著他們在華語樂壇主流評審體系裡的地位已經穩定。 樂評人厚安在《冬 未了》發行當下寫了一篇樂評，裡面有一句話被許多樂迷引用： > 「沒有答案，通常才是生命唯一的答案。」[^26] 樂評人吉暝水則在獨立媒體「立場新聞」上寫了一篇韋瓦第計畫的總評。他把整個系列放在台灣 indie 樂團「從獨立走向市場」這個討論的脈絡裡，認為獨立與市場之間的關係並不是非黑即白的二分法，包裝精美與音樂功夫也不是互斥的兩件事[^27]。 這句話後來成為韋瓦第計畫被理解的框架：蘇打綠沒有從獨立「轉去」主流，他們做的是把「獨立」跟「主流市場成功」這兩個被習慣對立起來的概念，直接拆掉那個對立。 韋瓦第計畫收尾後，蘇打綠在 2016 年下半年宣布「After Summer」告別巡演——五個城市、九場演出：北京（同名 + 小宇宙）、廣州（無與倫比的美麗 + 春日光）、香港（陪我歌唱 + 十年一刻）、上海（夏狂熱 + 你在煩惱什麼）、武漢（秋故事 + 冬未了）[^28]。每一場用兩張專輯曲目串起來作為主題。 蘇打綠用這場巡演對應了整個十二年的作品——從 2005 同名專輯到 2015《冬 未了》——而走向 2017 年那個沒有人預期會發生的時刻。
_〈無與倫比的美麗〉2007 年原版 MV — 收錄於同名專輯，蘇打綠第三張錄音室專輯，這張專輯裡的〈四季狂想〉是韋瓦第計畫的萌芽起點，也讓蘇打綠拿下 2008 年第 19 屆金曲獎最佳樂團。_
_2009 年蘇打綠在倫敦 Miloco Studios "The Pool" 錄製《夏／狂熱》的紀錄片段。Pink Floyd 的 Roger Waters 早年收購的傳奇錄音場地，後來由 Miloco 接管。一個台灣獨立樂團敢飛到這裡錄音的，在當時幾乎是孤例。_ --- ## 自由廣場的最後安可 ![2014 年「空氣中的視聽與幻覺」十週年世界巡迴的舞台前景，蘇打綠演出進行中](/article-images/music/sodagreen-vivaldi-tour-2014-09.jpg) _2014 年「空氣中的視聽與幻覺」十週年世界巡迴現場 — 從 2014 十週年到 2017 自由廣場最後安可，蘇打綠走完了主流盛期。Photo: Wikipedia user Solomon203, CC BY-SA 3.0. [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Sodagreen)。_ 2017 年 1 月 1 日，國家音樂廳。 那天蘇打綠跟 NSO 國家交響樂團合作了一場名為「樂計畫」的音樂會[^29]。這是蘇打綠成為第一個登上國家音樂廳開演唱會的台灣樂團——在那之前，國家音樂廳的舞台被默認為古典樂、戲曲、跨界藝術的場域，流行樂團不在這個名單上。 「樂計畫」結束之後，故事還沒完。當天晚上，蘇打綠在兩廳院藝文廣場（也就是現在大家熟悉的自由廣場）自掏腰包搭了一個舞台，開放給歌迷點歌——他們把這場稱為「最後安可」[^3]。場外估計約兩萬人。 吳青峰在那個舞台上對所有人喊了一句： > 「全宇宙的朋友大家好，我們是蘇打綠。」[^3] 這句話後來在 2023 年池堂影夜的小巨蛋會以另一個版本回來。但 2017 年 1 月 1 日當下，這句話是一個告別。 隔天，2017 年 1 月 2 日，蘇打綠正式宣布休團[^5]。 關於休團原因，當時官方的版本是「休團三年」——這個說法在 2016 年 10 月 1 日由林暐哲對外宣布[^30]。三年的休團，按計畫的話應該在 2019 年復出。 但後來的事情打亂了所有計畫。 2019 年下半年，蘇打綠團員嘗試以「魚丁糸」這個名字重新發表音樂的時候，跟林暐哲及其名下的公司之間的法律關係開始進入司法程序。2020 年起的訴訟過程裡，吳青峰陸續在庭外與訴狀中陳述：休團「是被林暐哲逼的」、「他希望我五年內不能參與蘇打綠」、「他要我脫團單飛被我斷然拒絕」[^31]。這些陳述屬於訴訟中事件的單方說法，是法律程序裡的指控與抗辯，這篇文章把它記錄為「訴訟中陳述」而不是史實。 對應地，林暐哲在 2022 年放棄商標的聲明裡是這樣描述當時的狀態的： > 「在疫情最嚴重的時候，我自主管理關在家裡，靜靜的思考，想起過去的點點滴滴。」[^32] 兩造的版本不一樣，這是訴訟中事件的常態。我們能確定的是：2017 年 1 月 2 日，蘇打綠宣布休團；2019 年 9 月 6 日，吳青峰發行了個人首張錄音室專輯《太空人》——這張專輯定位是休團期間個人作品的整理，不涉及脫團[^33]。 休團的官方說法、訴訟中單方陳述、林暐哲的事後回顧——三個版本指向不同的解釋，但它們有一個共同的時間錨：2017 年 1 月 1 日那場自由廣場的最後安可，是蘇打綠以「蘇打綠」這個名字走完的最後一步。下一次他們用這個名字站上小巨蛋，要等到 2023 年。
_2017 年 1 月 1 日，蘇打綠在自由廣場最後安可現場。約兩萬人。隔天宣布休團。_ --- ## 「我曾經視為父親的人」 休團之後的兩三年，蘇打綠團員嘗試以「魚丁糸」為名重新發表音樂。但「魚丁糸」與「蘇打綠」之間的法律關係，會在 2019 到 2021 年之間進入一連串的司法程序。 訴訟序列攤開來看是這樣的[^34]： | 階段 | 日期 | 內容 | 結果 | | ---------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | | 民事假處分 | 2019 年起 | 林暐哲先後兩次聲請 | 兩次均被駁回 | | 民事一審 | 2020-04-16 | 智慧財產法院 108 民著訴 134 號 | 駁回原告之訴 | | 刑事起訴 | 2020-02-24 | 北檢起訴吳青峰違反著作權法（範圍：2019/4-9 湖南衛視、7-11 高雄啤酒音樂節、澳門、師大附中、貢寮海祭、成都潮音節） | 案號北院 109 智訴 5 號 | | 民事二審 | 2021-04-01 | 智慧財產法院二審 | 駁回上訴（求償 500 萬全部敗訴；一審 800 萬→二審 500 萬） | | 刑事一審 | 2021-06-15 | 北院刑事一審判決 | 吳青峰、哈里坤的狂歡有限公司均無罪 | | 刑事上訴 | 2021-09-01 | 林暐哲方 | 放棄上訴 | 合計：林暐哲對吳青峰、民事四連敗加上刑事一敗，五連敗[^35]。自由時報報導裡提到「青峰累積創作 275 首」這個數字，不是訴狀指涉的侵權範圍——275 首是青峰在這個時間點為止的個人創作總數，跟訴訟範圍要分開[^36]。 2021 年 3 月 30 日，民事二審宣判前不久，吳青峰在法院外被媒體圍住。他說了這樣一段話： > 「我一向盲目相信的老師，一個我曾經視為父親的人，為什麼會變成這樣。」[^36] 這句話的關鍵字是「曾經視為」——過去式加上「視為」這個動詞，而不是「我把他當父親」這種現在式加上主觀視角的版本。許多後來轉述這段話的二手媒體把它寫成「我一直把他當父親」，但對照自由時報當天的記者實錄，原話是「我曾經視為父親的人」。這個差別不是修辭潔癖——「曾經視為」比「一直把他當」承載了更多重量，前者是一個關係的死亡證明，後者只是一個情感的宣告。 兩個月後，2021 年 5 月 11 日，吳青峰在 FB 上發了一篇 4000 字的公開聲明。這篇聲明在當時被多家媒體完整轉錄。聲明的開頭是這樣的： > 「我於理、於情都毫無虧欠，更是合理合法、仁至義盡。」[^37] 「於理、於情都毫無虧欠」這句話用了一個來自舊文言的雙鏡句，把法律論證跟情感論證放在同一個層次處理。後來部分二手媒體把這段濃縮成「我沒有一絲愧歉」用在標題，但「沒有一絲愧歉」並不是 FB 原文的逐字版本。這篇文章採用 FB 原文裡的「我於理、於情都毫無虧欠」作為引語。 這篇 4000 字聲明的結尾，則是這樣一句： > 「音樂一旦失去靈魂，就只剩下技巧；而法律一旦忽略人心，產生漏洞，就只是文字罷了。」[^37] 一個音樂人在打商標訴訟的時候，把音樂跟法律並列成兩個有可能失去人心的系統，這句話的重量在於它不是控訴對方，是控訴一整個讓人走到法庭上的結構。 > ⚠️ **爭議觀點**：2025 年 6 月，智慧財產法院一審判林暐哲應賠 2355 萬元，蘇打綠勝訴。但蘇打綠原訴指控的範圍是侵吞演唱會與版權收入近 4.7 億元[^38]。這場戰役從 2019 年的民事假處分聲請開始，走到 2025 年的智財判賠，已經超過六年——而且，還沒結束。判賠金額跟原訴指控金額之間的落差，是這個訴訟接下來幾年要繼續走完的部分。 --- ## 拆掉一個樂團的名字 2019 年 8 月 23 日，「魚丁糸有限公司」在經濟部辦理公司登記[^39]。2020 年 7 月 3 日，他們正式以樂團名義對外宣布以「魚丁糸」為新名字發表音樂；7 月 31 日辦了第一場「魚丁糸」名義的演唱會[^39]。 「魚丁糸」這個名字，是蘇打綠的拆字： - 蘇 = 艹 + 魚 + 禾 - 打 = 扌 + 丁 - 綠 = 糸 + 彔 取「魚」、「丁」、「糸」三個部首，組成「魚丁糸」。英文寫作 Oaeen。 這個拆字構想其實不是 2019 年才出現的。早在 2005 年蘇打綠第一張同名專輯發行前，他們在試聽片上就用過這個名字。2016 年，當蘇打綠正在 After Summer 告別巡演的時候，他們也曾以「魚丁糸」這個分身名登過海洋音樂祭的小舞台——那是一場用「分身名」回到一切開始的場地的儀式性表演。 六位團員的「魚丁糸」分身藝名是這樣的[^40]： | 本名 | 魚丁糸分身 | | ---- | ---------- | | 青峰 | 日出 | | 馨儀 | 香我 | | 小威 | 八女 | | 阿福 | 可田 | | 家凱 | 豕豆 | | 阿龔 | 金八 | 每個藝名都是本名某個部首的重組或變形——「青峰 → 日出」用青字裡的「日」加上峰的意象；「馨儀 → 香我」用馨字裡的「香」加上儀的對稱；「小威 → 八女」是直接的拆字。歌迷代號叫「浮萍」，歌迷會叫「池堂」——「池堂影夜」這個 2023 年小巨蛋演唱會的名字，就是從歌迷會「池堂」延伸來的。 「魚丁糸」的存在遠超過替代品的層次。它是一個拆字、一個分身、一個抗議——當「蘇打綠」這三個字因為商標歸屬的問題沒辦法用的時候，他們把這三個字拆開、把部首挑出來、重新組合成另一個名字，繼續做音樂。 > 📝 **策展人筆記**：把「魚丁糸」放在台灣 indie 與抗爭符號的脈絡裡看，會發現它的精神跟 1990 年代之後台灣社運常用的「諧音改名」、「拆字改名」傳統相通——名字本身就是抗爭的載體。差別在於：社運的拆字常常是把對方的名字拆得難聽（例如把官方名稱改成貶義），魚丁糸是把自己原來的名字拆開保留，是一個「我把我自己的名字暫時藏起來但你看得出來」的做法。這個動作不挑釁，但很堅定——一個被剝奪了原名的樂團，用拆字的方式繼續說「我們還在」。 魚丁糸成立後，他們啟動了一個更大的計畫：把蘇打綠時期的所有作品「復刻」重錄。 復刻計畫的第一張，是蘇打綠首張同名專輯的魚丁糸版本，命名為《不同名專輯》[^41]。2022 年 1 月 14 日數位首發，2 月 18 日實體首發。第二張是《小宇宙（魚版）》同步推出。 復刻計畫的設定是：每張復刻專輯以「雙倍預算」錄製「雙倍曲目」。原始的 11 首歌會被擴充成 22 首，部分是舊曲新編，部分是同期未發表的 B-side。整個計畫預計重新錄製超過 200 首歌曲[^41]——這個數字是魚丁糸自己對外公布的目標，跟訴訟範圍的「275 首」要分開。 韋瓦第四季的復刻也在計畫裡，按進度延續到 2025 年。 復刻計畫的意義在這裡：當「蘇打綠」這個名字在 2019 到 2022 年之間還沒回到他們手上的時候，他們用「魚丁糸」的名義把蘇打綠時期的所有歌重新唱一次、重新編一次、重新錄一次。「魚丁糸」成為一個讓蘇打綠的歌可以繼續在現場被唱、可以繼續被購買、可以繼續產生收入的法律外殼——而且，每一次魚丁糸唱蘇打綠的歌，就等於是把這些歌跟原來的法律歸屬切割出去一點點。 --- ## 「該放下了」與「我們是蘇打綠」 2022 年 5 月 30 日——蘇打綠的成團日，也就是 2004 年那場政大 School Rock 發行《空氣中的視聽與幻覺》的同一個日期——林暐哲在 FB 上發了一篇聲明，宣布放棄「蘇打綠 Sodagreen」的商標所有權。 聲明的關鍵段落是這樣的[^32][^42]： > 「這個事情走到今天，有一個內在的聲音告訴我『該放下了』。」 > > 「在疫情最嚴重的時候，我自主管理關在家裡，靜靜的思考，想起過去的點點滴滴。我知道該做一個決定，放下過去，不管好的壞的都一樣。所以我決定放棄商標的所有權。並祝福這個名字有更好的未來。」 > > 「法律有助於釐清事實，卻也把人與人之間的感情和信任摧毀了。」 > > 「Life is short，希望大家健康平安，祝福你們。」 「該放下了」這三個字，被廣泛引用。但完整的脈絡是「這個事情走到今天，有一個內在的聲音告訴我『該放下了』」——一個訴訟五連敗的當事人，把放棄商標的決定描述為一個「內在聲音」的指引。這篇聲明被中央社、Blow、Marie Claire 等多家媒體完整刊登。 商標的法律處理過程不像聲明那樣戲劇化。林暐哲拋棄的「蘇打綠 Sodagreen」相關商標共六件——其中五件是林暐哲音樂社在 2007 到 2008 年間申請、2013 年移轉給公司持有的；另一件是 2013 年 3 月由公司直接申請的[^43]。林暐哲放棄商標不等於蘇打綠團員自動取得——他放棄之後，需要有人去重新申請。最後的結果是：經濟部智慧財產局（TIPO）於 2022 年 10 月 1 日公告，「蘇打綠」團名由「蘇打綠有限公司」取得商標權，為期 10 年[^44]。 2023 年 2 月 18 日。台北小巨蛋。 蘇打綠以「魚丁糸」名義舉辦的演唱會，名稱叫《池堂影夜》——「池堂」是歌迷會的代號，「影夜」是夜晚的意象。兩場演出，兩萬名觀眾[^1]。當主唱站上舞台、深吸一口氣的時候，他喊出來的是： > 「全宇宙的朋友大家好，我們是『蘇打綠』！」[^1] 這句話在 2017 年自由廣場最後安可的時候出現過一次。那一次是告別。2023 年池堂影夜小巨蛋這一次，是回來。 三個月後，2023 年 5 月 30 日——又是 5 月 30 日——蘇打綠在中正紀念堂舉辦《蘇打綠 Round 2》免費演唱會。吳青峰在台上說了一句[^45]： > 「等待很久了，6 年吧，終於可以說出來，我們是蘇打綠。」 「6 年」這個數字，是從 2017 年 1 月 2 日宣布休團那天算到 2023 年。 回歸之後，蘇打綠的國際巡演重新啟動。2024 年 3 月 22-24 日，香港紅磡體育館三場——這是《二十年一刻》巡迴復出的首站[^46]。2025 年 4 月 20 日，倫敦 OVO Arena Wembley——這是蘇打綠第一次在倫敦舉辦 arena 等級的演唱會。2025 年 5 月 29 日，東京[^47]。 > 💡 **你知道嗎？** 蘇打綠的首場海外演出是 2007 年 8 月 11 日，在新加坡共和理工學院文化中心舉辦的「今年夏天特別綠」演唱會[^48]。從新加坡到倫敦 Wembley Arena，這條海外巡演的弧線走了將近十八年。 --- ## 學費 4.7 億：台灣 indie 樂團的商標課 2025 年 6 月，智慧財產法院一審宣判：林暐哲應賠蘇打綠 2355 萬元[^38]。蘇打綠原訴指控的範圍——侵吞演唱會與版權收入——估算近 4.7 億元。判賠 2355 萬跟原訴指控 4.7 億之間的差距，是訴訟程序接下來幾年還要繼續走完的部分。林暐哲方面是否上訴、二審結果如何，這篇文章封稿時尚未有定論。 蘇打綠的整個訴訟過程，被台灣法律圈廣泛討論。多家律師事務所——著作權筆記、tandemlaw、巨群法律所、khcattorney（杜昀浩律師）、weleadlaw——都寫過這個案子的案例分析[^49][^50]。從這些分析裡可以歸納出三個法律界的共識： 第一，**台灣的商標法採註冊主義**。誰先去 TIPO（經濟部智慧財產局）登記，誰就持有商標權，跟「誰先用這個名字」「誰是這個名字的代表」沒有直接關係。蘇打綠 2003 年就用這個名字演出，但因為一直到 2007 年才由林暐哲音樂社申請商標，先註冊的就是林暐哲音樂社。 第二，**經紀合約如果沒有就「商標歸屬」白紙黑字約定，商標屬於註冊人**。也就是經紀公司或廠牌，而不是藝人。蘇打綠當年跟林暐哲音樂社的合約，並沒有明確處理商標權的歸屬條款。 第三，**樂團應該在出道時就申請商標**——這是法律界對所有 indie 樂團的一致建議。商標申請費用不貴，過程也不複雜，但它能避免十年、十五年、二十年後一場曠日廢時的法律戰。 杜昀浩律師在他的案例分析裡特別指出一點：二審判決的關鍵並非「網友能不能叫出團員全名」這種知名度認定，而是基於商標法的「註冊主義」原則[^51]。這個提醒值得記下來，因為許多新聞報導把這個案子簡化成「網友支不支持」、「市場認不認得」的辯論，但法庭的邏輯不是這樣運作的。 從蘇打綠這場二十年的訴訟裡，台灣 indie 音樂工業整體上學到了什麼？ 最直接的影響是：2022 到 2025 年之間，台灣許多 indie 樂團主動去 TIPO 申請商標的數量明顯上升。法律事務所對音樂產業的諮詢需求也上升。「樂團出道前先申請商標」這件事，從一個過去被認為「太商業化、不夠 indie」的動作，變成一個基本的自我保護。 從這個角度看，蘇打綠四年的訴訟，付出的不只是他們自己的時間、金錢、情感——也是台灣 indie 樂團集體的一次法律教育。4.7 億的訴訟金額、2355 萬的判賠、六年半的法庭程序，是他們替整個產業學的一堂課。 ✦ **台灣 indie 樂團出道第一件事，是先把團名註冊起來。** --- ## 「我們是『蘇打綠』！」 2023 年 2 月 18 日，小巨蛋的舞台上，吳青峰深吸一口氣，喊出：「全宇宙的朋友大家好，我們是『蘇打綠』！」 兩萬個座位的觀眾一起哭出來。 距離 2001 年那個政大金旋獎舞台、距離他們組起樂團那天，整整二十二年。 那個高三時拿天韻獎、大一就跟學長學姊組樂團報名校園歌唱比賽的男孩，現在依然在唱。只是這一次，他不再需要任何人的允許，就能唱出自己的〈小情歌〉。 --- **延伸閱讀**： - [張懸與安溥](/music/張懸與安溥) — 同期 indie 女聲，獨立樂團集體的政治表態 - [台灣獨立音樂](/music/台灣獨立音樂) — 台灣 indie 樂團生態 - --- title: 'Sodagreen: It Took Them Twenty Years to Learn That Even Their Own Name Would End Up in Court' description: "In 2001, four students formed a band on the NCCU Golden Melody stage; in 2003, they were discovered by Lin Wei-che at the Gongliao Ocean Music Festival; they performed Vivaldi's four seasons across Beijing and Berlin; in 2019, they were sued; in 2023, they finally reclaimed their name at Taipei Arena — Sodagreen walked twenty years to learn this: the first thing a Taiwan indie band should do upon debut is register the band name." date: 2026-06-09 author: 'Taiwan.md Contributors' category: 'Music' subcategory: '獨立與搖滾' tags: ['蘇打綠', '魚丁糸', '吳青峰', '台灣音樂', '著作權', '韋瓦第計畫', '林暐哲'] readingTime: 28 lastVerified: 2026-06-09 lastHumanReview: true featured: false translatedFrom: 'Music/蘇打綠.md' sourceCommitSha: '73443b2a4' sourceContentHash: 'sha256:5156eab09e028ec3' sourceBodyHash: 'sha256:fbb2b0f8546f4d0c' translatedAt: '2026-06-12T03:51:13+08:00' --- # Sodagreen: It Took Them Twenty Years to Learn That Even Their Own Name Would End Up in Court > **30-second overview:** In May 2001, four students took the stage at the NCCU Golden Melody Awards with a song called "Kui" and won the Band Category's Most Popular Award. That band was called "Sodagreen." Twenty-two years later at Taipei Arena, lead singer Wu Qingfeng took a deep breath and shouted, "We are 'Sodagreen'!" — declaring the end of a four-year legal battle[^1]. In between: the Vivaldi Project carried them through London, Beijing, Berlin, and Taitung; at the 27th Golden Melody Awards, _Winter Endless_ swept five trophies in one night[^2]; a final encore at Liberty Plaza drew a crowd of twenty thousand[^3]; and after 2019 came a cascade of alter-ego acts under the name "Oaeen," trademark lawsuits, criminal indictments, and Lin Wei-che's five consecutive courtroom defeats[^4]. This is the story of a band that walked from a student club into a courtroom, and from the courtroom back to the arena. In May 2001, at the National Chengchi University (NCCU) gymnasium. On the stage of the 18th NCCU Golden Melody Awards finals, four students stood together. Lead singer Wu Qingfeng, a freshman in the Chinese literature department; bassist Xie Xinyi, business administration; drummer Shi Junwei, sociology; guitarist "Xin-ge," a high school friend of Qingfeng's[^5]. Their name was "Sodagreen." That day, with a song called "Kui," they won the Band Category's Most Popular Award[^5]. No one imagined that twenty-two years later, when the lead singer shouted "We are 'Sodagreen'!" from the stage of Taipei Arena, he was declaring the end of a four-year legal battle[^1]. That was the twenty-year arc of a band walking from a student club to a courtroom, and from the courtroom back to the arena. --- ## "Kui": The Band Category's First Place at the NCCU Golden Melody Awards The 18th NCCU Golden Melody Awards took place in 2001. The venue was the NCCU gymnasium, organized by the university's Student Activities Division and student clubs[^6]. The Golden Melody Awards hold a place in the history of Taiwan's campus music competitions — alongside NTNU's Teacher's Cup and FJU's Fu Jen Star, they are considered the three major campus music awards in Taiwan, running from 1984 to 2026, now in their fortieth year. In the early years they carried the lingering warmth of the folk song movement; around 2000 they transformed into an incubator for indie bands: Cheer Chen, Sticky Rice, Natural Q, and Sodagreen were all heard by the music industry from this stage[^6]. Four students forming a band for the first time needed a song to compete with. Wu Qingfeng brought out "Kui," a song he had written in his senior year of high school. The song's origin predated the competition itself: in 1999, at the Affiliated Senior High School of National Taiwan Normal University's campus "Tianyun Awards" songwriting category, Wu Qingfeng won first place with "Kui"[^7]. He was seventeen. On the day of the Golden Melody Awards finals, they performed "Kui." They took home the Band Category's Most Popular Award[^5][^7]. The "Soda" in Sodagreen was drummer Shi Junwei's idea — the refreshing sensation of fizzy bubbles[^8]. "Green" was Wu Qingfeng's favorite color. The band name was assembled that way, with no profound allusion. In later interviews they admitted it was simply catchy and easy to remember[^8]. > 💡 **Did you know?** Compared to many deliberately conceptual band names, "Sodagreen" is just two members' preferences layered on top of each other — one contributed a flavor, the other a color. The name had no deep meaning, which paradoxically made it a vessel onto which all kinds of interpretations could later be projected. Twenty years later, when the name entered a trademark courtroom, no one argued about "what these three characters originally meant" — the argument was over "who registered it first." From May 2001 to March 2003, nearly two years, Sodagreen was unstable. The guitarist position rotated through three people: Yanting, Xiaowei, and Hongru[^5]. A student band with constantly changing members was the most common thing in the campus music scene — members studying, interning, finishing military service, living across different campuses, all pulling at whether a band could sustain itself. Wu Qingfeng's own academic trajectory: after graduating from Kaohsiung Municipal Kaohsiung Senior High School, he entered National Chengchi University's Chinese literature department through a recommendation-based admissions process in 2000[^9]. The NCCU Memory Network — a university history wiki constructed by the NCCU Library — preserved this record of his admission. During his time at NCCU, he didn't confine himself to the Chinese department alone; he dabbled in radio broadcasting, choir, and record design. Sodagreen's early identity was not rooted in the "NCCU campus folk song" tradition but was closer to the wave of indie bands emerging from Taiwan's university campuses in the late 1990s to early 2000s — labels not necessarily in place, studios not necessarily available, but every campus had a band, and every band was finding its footing on the competition stage. Xie Xinyi was among the first friends Wu Qingfeng made at NCCU. She studied business administration, and she and Shi Junwei were junior and senior members of the NCCU swimming team — this was an era of layered campus community connections, where bands typically formed through the same group of people linking from one club activity to another and then assembling, a completely different path from the professional model of "the music industry has a need and then goes looking for people"[^5]. March 2003 was an important milestone in Sodagreen's history. That month, three new members joined one after another: He Jingyang (NCCU Graduate Institute of Public Administration, acoustic guitar, later became the band leader, nicknamed Afu), Liu Jiakai (NCCU Department of Psychology, electric guitar), and Gong Yuqi (Taipei National University of the Arts Graduate Institute of Music, piano and viola, nicknamed Agong)[^5][^10]. The original four plus these three established Sodagreen's six-member lineup. But there is a small historical discrepancy here. Chinese Wikipedia records He Jingyang's joining date as March 2003, but He Jingyang himself said in a later Yahoo interview that it was "on my birthday in 2004 when Qingfeng sent me a message" that he joined[^10]. The two versions differ by a year, and to date there is no authoritative account that reconciles this discrepancy. That members' memories of a joining date could diverge so much may reflect one thing: during that period, "Sodagreen" was a fluid name rather than a fixed roster — the boundary between who counted as an official member and who was just helping out for one show was blurry. > 📝 **Curator's note:** The six members were spread across the Chinese literature department, business administration, sociology, the Graduate Institute of Public Administration, psychology, and the TNUA Graduate Institute of Music — five departments across two universities. This disciplinary diversity would later be amplified in the Vivaldi Project: a band daring to cross from folk to rock to traditional Chinese instrumentation to classical grandeur may well have been related to the fact that its six members included someone studying sociology, someone studying musicology, someone studying public administration, and someone studying psychology. The word "band" is often imagined as "a group of music majors," but Sodagreen was a counterexample from the very beginning. --- ## Chinese Literature, Business Administration, Sociology, TNUA Music Graduate Institute Laying out the six members' academic backgrounds produces an interesting picture: **Wu Qingfeng** (lead singer, songwriter), born August 30, 1982, from Kaohsiung; graduated from the Affiliated Senior High School of NTNU; NCCU Chinese literature department; studied abroad at the University of Birmingham in the UK for one year starting in 2004. Beginning with his high school Tianyun Awards win for "Kui," his output of lyrics and compositions far exceeded that of a typical rock band frontman[^9]. **Xie Xinyi** (bass), born April 16, 1982, NCCU business administration; one of the four founding members; in many interviews she was positioned as the "band's household manager" — handling administration, scheduling, and communication. **Shi Junwei** (drums), born August 26, 1979, NCCU sociology department. Yamaha lists him on their Taiwan artists page; he is one of the few Sodagreen members with a professional equipment endorsement relationship[^11]. The word "Soda" was his suggestion. **He Jingyang** (acoustic guitar, band leader), born April 4, 1982, NCCU Graduate Institute of Public Administration. He later took on the role of Sodagreen's band leader. **Liu Jiakai** (electric guitar), born February 5, 1982, NCCU Department of Psychology. **Gong Yuqi** (piano, viola), born December 16, 1980, TNUA Graduate Institute of Music — the only member with a formal music degree. He was the one who, during the Vivaldi Project's _Winter Endless_, secluded himself for three months and single-handedly completed a 70-person orchestral score[^12]. Lining up the six disciplines: Chinese, business administration, sociology, public administration, psychology, musicology. Three departments in management and policy, two in humanities and social sciences, one in music. This combination would be considered "atypical" for any indie band. The story of a band's formation tends to be undervalued at this level. The common narrative framework for Taiwan indie bands is "a group of students who love music got together" — but Sodagreen's group originated from the same time period on the NCCU campus, people who knew each other, someone suggesting they sign up for a campus competition. The campus competition was the first motivation; the music came later. This difference in sequence may seem small, but it affects the entire story that follows. A band that starts from a campus competition and a band that starts from a recording demo relate to the music industry differently. The former is accustomed to the loop of "take the stage, get heard"; the latter is accustomed to the loop of "enter the studio, collaborate with a producer." When Sodagreen was later taken under Lin Wei-che's wing and entered the mainstream, they had already been practicing the "take the stage, get heard" instinct for two or three years — an instinct that couldn't be captured in a demo. --- ## "This Is the One": The Hot Wave Rock Stage at Gongliao Ocean Music Festival ![March 12, 2008 — Sodagreen performing live at The Wall in Taipei, lead singer Wu Qingfeng on stage with five bandmates](/article-images/music/sodagreen-the-wall-2008.jpg) _March 12, 2008 — Sodagreen live at The Wall, Taipei. Photo: Wikipedia user Solomon203, CC BY-SA 3.0. [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sodagreen-20080312TheWall.jpg)._ July 2003. Fulong Beach, Gongliao District, New Taipei City. The 4th Ocean Music Festival. Sodagreen was still a student band with no album. They signed up for the festival's "Hot Wave Rock" stage — this stage was not on the same tier as the main stage; Hot Wave Rock was a smaller stage for campus and indie bands[^5]. That festival, they were part of the "Yin Xia Tian" farewell tour: they had already played at Ximen, Tainan Wutuobang, Kaohsiung ATT Secret Base, Taichung Laonuo, and Taipei University Expo[^5]. A standard small-venue island-wide tour for a student band — nothing unusual. But in the audience that day was a man named Lin Wei-che. Lin Wei-che was by then a veteran producer in Taiwan's music scene. He had been the lead singer of the band Baboo in his early years, moved behind the scenes in the 1990s, and produced for Cheer Chen, Faith Yang, Deserts Chang, and Sodagreen[^13]. Under that small Hot Wave Rock stage at Gongliao, he heard Wu Qingfeng's voice. He later described his judgment in an interview with _Business Today_ in a single sentence: > "This is the one."[^13] This moment has been quoted repeatedly, but the details surrounding it are often oversimplified. A more accurate context is this: people in the music industry who had heard Wu Qingfeng's demo had polarized reactions to that voice. Some found it "neither male nor female"; others found it unique. Lin Wei-che later reframed this controversy into a warmer phrase — "now-male, now-female" — and wrote it into Sodagreen's album copy[^14]. The word "now" (忽) is several degrees gentler than "neither" (不). Lin Wei-che didn't fully accept that voice in one go. In later interviews he recalled that when he first heard Wu Qingfeng's voice, he was actually somewhat repulsed in his mind, but his body was more honest than his mind — his body listened to the entire song before he realized he had already been drawn in. The original source of this account has been passed around the internet several times; a complete version can only be found in secondhand retellings — so this article treats it as background context rather than a direct quotation. Lin Wei-che decided to sign Sodagreen, bringing them under his independent label, "Lin Wei-che Music Co." The significance of this decision was not apparent at the time, but it would determine Sodagreen's relationship with the mainstream market for the next decade or more — and twenty years later, this decision would come back to question everyone in a different form. On May 30, 2004, Sodagreen released their first official music at the "School Rock" concert held at NCCU[^15]. Sharing the stage were Cheer Chen and Deserts Chang — two representatives of Taiwan's indie female voices at the time. Sodagreen sharing the stage with them was equivalent to being formally introduced into this circle. What they released was a two-track single, published by Lin Wei-che Music Co., the first official commercial CD since Sodagreen's founding. Lin Wei-che Music Co. later designated May 30 as "Sodagreen Day" — a date that continued through to 2022, when Lin Wei-che published his statement relinquishing the trademark on Facebook, and he also chose May 30[^16]. On September 3, 2005, Sodagreen released their self-titled debut album _Sodagreen_. Produced by Lin Wei-che Music Co., with Lin Wei-che as producer, 11 tracks, 53 minutes and 40 seconds[^17]. The pre-order bonus was a copy of _Sodazine 1_ — a unique early Sodagreen tradition where each album came with a zine compiled by the members themselves, containing lyrics, writing backgrounds, photos, and casual notes. This zine series later became a collector's item among fans. The debut album was nominated for Best Band at the 17th Golden Melody Awards. They didn't win, but they had been noticed by the Golden Melody system. > 📝 **Curator's note:** The existence of "Lin Wei-che Music Co." as an independent label had meaning in Taiwan's indie music industry of that era. For indie bands at the time, there were a few paths to surviving within the mainstream system: get signed by one of the five major record companies, collaborate with a mainstream management company, or figure it out on your own. "Lin Wei-che Music Co." took a variant of the third path: a veteran producer with mainstream production experience opened a small label and brought in the bands he believed in. This was a relatively avant-garde path at the time, but it also planted a seed of a problem: when the producer is simultaneously the label owner, the contracting party, and the trademark holder, the power dynamic between artist and producer is more intimate and more ambiguous than the typical "artist vs. company" relationship.
_"Little Love Song" — lead single from the October 2006 album *Small Universe*. Written entirely by Wu Qingfeng, published by Lin Wei-che Music Co., it later won Best Composition at the 18th Golden Melody Awards in 2007._ --- ## Vivaldi Took Them to London, Beijing, Berlin ![July 2014 — "Audiovisuals and Hallucinations in the Air" 10th Anniversary World Tour at Taipei Arena, Sodagreen performing under stage lights](/article-images/music/sodagreen-vivaldi-tour-2014-09.jpg) _July 2014 — "Audiovisuals and Hallucinations in the Air" 10th Anniversary World Tour, Taipei Arena, three consecutive shows. Photo: Wikipedia user Solomon203, CC BY-SA 3.0. [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Sodagreen)._ On October 20, 2006, Sodagreen released their second studio album, _Small Universe_[^18]. The title track and "Little Love Song" were the two defining tracks of the album. The lyrics and music for "Little Love Song" were entirely Wu Qingfeng's work; the melody's cleanliness and the lyrics' colloquialism pushed the song onto the must-sing list for Taiwanese college students in that era's KTVs. At the 18th Golden Melody Awards in 2007, Sodagreen won Best Band for _Small Universe_, and "Little Love Song" won Best Composer (Wu Qingfeng)[^2]. From being discovered by Lin Wei-che at the 2003 Gongliao Ocean Music Festival to winning their first Golden Melody for Best Band, less than four years had passed. The third studio album, released the same year, _Incomparable Beauty_, contained a song called "Four Seasons Rhapsody." This song was later identified as the seed of the Vivaldi Project: the lyrics and melody already carried the embryonic concept of the cycle of seasons, but it had not yet been expanded into a multi-year album series[^19]. At the 19th Golden Melody Awards in 2008, Sodagreen won Best Band again for _Incomparable Beauty_[^2]. Winning Best Band two years in a row meant they had moved from "newcomers noticed by the Golden Melody circle" to "a band confirmed by the Golden Melody circle." What followed was the Vivaldi Project. The Vivaldi Project — named after Baroque composer Antonio Vivaldi's _The Four Seasons_ violin concertos — was a relatively ambitious undertaking in the history of Mandarin pop music. It comprised **four seasons, four cities, four moods, four albums**[^20]. The entire series began with the first album in 2009 and concluded with the last in 2015, with two non-Vivaldi albums released in between, spanning a total of six and a half years. The timeline: **Spring — Sunlight** — Released May 8, 2009, Sodagreen's fifth studio album, theme city: Taitung, musical style: "folk spring"[^21]. The recording team swapped the large studio for Taitung's natural environment, integrating it with local musical textures. This was the first Vivaldi Project album and the one that anchored the entire series' concept. **Summer / Fever** — Released September 11, 2009, the sixth studio album, recorded at London's Miloco Studios "The Pool" — a legendary recording space acquired in the 1970s by Pink Floyd's Roger Waters and later taken over by Miloco. Sodagreen's expenditure for recording the album there was reported to be close to NT$10 million[^22]. Musical style: rock and Britpop. **Autumn: Story** — Released September 18, 2013, the ninth studio album, theme city: Beijing, musical style: "poetic Chinese folk," with heavy use of traditional Chinese instruments such as the guzheng, erhu, and suona in the arrangements[^23]. There was a full four years between Summer and Autumn — during which Sodagreen released a non-Validi album _What Are You Worried About_ (November 11, 2011, the eighth), while Afu (He Jingyang) served military service and Agong (Gong Yuqi) also faced conscription arrangements, forcing the autumn and winter plans to be pushed back[^5]. **Winter Endless** — Released November 4, 2015, the tenth studio album, theme city: Berlin, musical style: "solemn classical," recorded in collaboration with the German Pops Orchestra (a 60-person symphony orchestra)[^24]. Four albums, four cities, four seasons, four musical styles. For an indie band to plan a six-and-a-half-year conceptual series of this scope, there is no other comparison in Taiwan's indie history. flipermag, in a 2016 comprehensive review of the Vivaldi Project, summed it up in one sentence: > "'Spring' in Taitung belongs to warm folk, 'Summer' in London belongs to passionate rock, 'Autumn' in Beijing belongs to melancholy poetry, 'Winter' in Berlin belongs to solemn classical."[^20] The production details of _Winter Endless_ deserve a special section. Agong — Gong Yuqi, the only member with a formal music degree — secluded himself for three months and single-handedly completed a 70-person orchestral score[^12]. During the Berlin recording, they collaborated with the German Pops Orchestra's 60-person symphony. A Taiwan indie band recording an entire album with a professional overseas symphony orchestra was an extremely rare undertaking at the time. > 💡 **Did you know?** The second Vivaldi Project album, _Summer / Fever_, was recorded at London's Miloco Studios at a reported cost of close to NT$10 million[^22]. For an indie band to spend NT$10 million recording an album in London was almost counterintuitive within Taiwan's indie music industry at the time — most indie bands' recording budgets for an album were less than a tenth of that figure. Calling the Vivaldi Project a "luxury" is not an overstatement, but that luxury produced results: six and a half years later, the 27th Golden Melody Awards handed five trophies to _Winter Endless_ in one night. The results of the 27th Golden Melody Awards in 2016 were the official coronation of the entire Vivaldi Project series: - Best Mandarin Album: **_Winter Endless_** - Best Band: **Sodagreen** - Best Album Producer: **Lin Wei-che** - Best Lyricist: **Wu Qingfeng** ("He Raised His Right Hand and Called the Roll") - Best Arranger: **Sodagreen + Lin Wei-che** ("A Gorgeous Sorrow")[^25] One album, five Golden Melody Awards — this achievement is a rare case in the history of Taiwan's Golden Melody Awards. From the 18th Golden Melody Awards in 2007 to the 27th in 2016, Sodagreen won Best Band three times — a band winning Best Band three times at the Golden Melody Awards means their standing within the mainstream judging system of the Chinese-language music world had solidified. Music critic Hou-an wrote a review of _Winter Endless_ at the time of its release, containing a line that has been widely quoted by fans: > "Having no answer is usually life's only answer."[^26] Music critic Jimingshui wrote a comprehensive review of the Vivaldi Project for the independent media outlet _Stand News_. Placing the entire series within the discussion of Taiwan indie bands "moving from independence to the market," he argued that the relationship between independence and the market is not a black-and-white dichotomy, and that polished packaging and musical craft are not mutually exclusive[^27]. This line later became the framework through which the Vivaldi Project was understood: Sodagreen didn't "switch" from indie to mainstream — they dismantled the habitual opposition between "indie" and "mainstream market success." After the Vivaldi Project concluded, Sodagreen announced the "After Summer" farewell tour in the second half of 2016 — five cities, nine shows: Beijing (_Sodagreen_ + _Small Universe_), Guangzhou (_Incomparable Beauty_ + _Spring Sunlight_), Hong Kong (_Sing with Me_ + _A Decade's Moment_), Shanghai (_Summer Fever_ + _What Are You Worried About_), Wuhan (_Autumn Story_ + _Winter Endless_)[^28]. Each show was themed around two albums' setlists. Sodagreen used this tour to correspond with their entire body of work across twelve years — from the 2005 self-titled album to 2015's _Winter Endless_ — and walked toward the moment in 2017 that no one expected would happen.
_"Incomparable Beauty" 2007 original MV — from the album of the same name, Sodagreen's third studio album. "Four Seasons Rhapsody" on this album was the germinal starting point of the Vivaldi Project, and it also earned Sodagreen the 19th Golden Melody Award for Best Band in 2008._
_2009 — footage of Sodagreen recording *Summer / Fever* at London's Miloco Studios "The Pool." A legendary recording space acquired by Pink Floyd's Roger Waters in his early years, later taken over by Miloco. A Taiwan indie band daring to fly here to record was virtually a singular case at the time._ --- ## The Last Encore at Liberty Plaza ![2014 — "Audiovisuals and Hallucinations in the Air" 10th Anniversary World Tour stage foreground, Sodagreen mid-performance](/article-images/music/sodagreen-vivaldi-tour-2014-09.jpg) _2014 — "Audiovisuals and Hallucinations in the Air" 10th Anniversary World Tour live — from the 10th anniversary in 2014 to the last encore at Liberty Plaza in 2017, Sodagreen completed their mainstream heyday. Photo: Wikipedia user Solomon203, CC BY-SA 3.0. [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Sodagreen)._ January 1, 2017. National Concert Hall. That day, Sodagreen collaborated with the National Symphony Orchestra (NSO) on a concert called "Le Project"[^29]. This made Sodagreen the first Taiwan band to perform a concert at the National Concert Hall — prior to that, the National Concert Hall stage was implicitly reserved for classical music, traditional opera, and crossover arts; pop bands were not on that list. After "Le Project" ended, the story wasn't over. That evening, Sodagreen paid out of pocket to set up a stage at the National Theater and Concert Hall Arts Plaza (what everyone now knows as Liberty Plaza), opening it up for fans to request songs — they called this the "Last Encore"[^3]. An estimated twenty thousand people gathered outside. Wu Qingfeng shouted to everyone from that stage: > "Hello, friends across the universe — we are Sodagreen."[^3] This line would return in another form at the 2023 Pond of Shadows Night concert at Taipei Arena. But on January 1, 2017, in that moment, it was a farewell. The next day, January 2, 2017, Sodagreen officially announced their hiatus[^5]. Regarding the reason for the hiatus, the official version at the time was "a three-year break" — this statement was announced to the public by Lin Wei-che on October 1, 2016[^30]. The three-year hiatus was supposed to end with a comeback in 2019. But subsequent events disrupted all plans. In the second half of 2019, when Sodagreen members attempted to release new music under the name "Oaeen," the legal relationship between them and Lin Wei-che and his companies entered judicial proceedings. During the litigation process beginning in 2020, Wu Qingfeng stated in and out of court: the hiatus "was forced by Lin Wei-che," "he wanted me to not participate in Sodagreen for five years," "he wanted me to leave the band and go solo, which I flatly refused"[^31]. These statements represent one side's account of events during litigation — allegations and defenses within legal proceedings. This article records them as "statements made during litigation" rather than established fact. Correspondingly, in his 2022 statement relinquishing the trademark, Lin Wei-che described the situation at the time as follows: > "During the worst of the pandemic, I self-isolated at home, quietly reflecting, thinking back on all the moments past and present."[^32] The two sides' versions differ — this is normal in litigation. What we can confirm is: on January 2, 2017, Sodagreen announced their hiatus; on September 6, 2019, Wu Qingfeng released his first solo studio album _Spaceman_ — positioned as a collection of personal works during the hiatus, not a departure from the band[^33]. The official hiatus narrative, one-sided statements during litigation, Lin Wei-che's retrospective account — three versions point to different explanations, but they share a common temporal anchor: the last encore at Liberty Plaza on January 1, 2017, was the last step Sodagreen took under the name "Sodagreen." The next time they would use that name on the stage of Taipei Arena would be 2023.
_January 1, 2017 — Sodagreen at the Liberty Plaza Last Encore. Approximately twenty thousand people. The next day, they announced their hiatus._ --- ## "Someone I Once Regarded as a Father" In the two or three years following the hiatus, Sodagreen members attempted to release new music under the name "Oaeen." But the legal relationship between "Oaeen" and "Sodagreen" would enter a series of judicial proceedings between 2019 and 2021. The litigation sequence unfolded as follows[^34]: | Stage | Date | Content | Result | | ---------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Civil provisional injunction | From 2019 | Lin Wei-che filed two applications | Both were dismissed | | Civil first instance | 2020-04-16 | Intellectual Property Court Case No. 108-Min-Zhu-Su-134 | Plaintiff's claim dismissed | | Criminal indictment | 2020-02-24 | Taipei District Prosecutors Office indicted Wu Qingfeng for copyright infringement (scope: Hunan TV April–September 2019, Kaohsiung Beer Music Festival July–11, Macau, Affiliated High School of NTNU, Gongliao Ocean Music Festival, Chengdu Chaoyin Festival) | Case No. Bei-Yuan-109-Zhi-Su-5 | | Civil second instance | 2021-04-01 | Intellectual Property Court appeal | Appeal dismissed (NT$5 million damages claim entirely lost; first instance NT$8 million → second instance NT$5 million) | | Criminal first instance | 2021-06-15 | Taipei District Court criminal first instance verdict | Wu Qingfeng and Harry Kun's Carnival Co., Ltd. both acquitted | | Criminal appeal | 2021-09-01 | Lin Wei-che's side | Abandoned appeal | In total: Lin Wei-che suffered four consecutive civil losses plus one criminal loss against Wu Qingfeng — five consecutive defeats[^35]. The _Liberty Times_ report mentioning "Qingfeng has written a cumulative 275 songs" is not within the scope of the infringement alleged in the complaint — 275 is the total number of songs Qingfeng had written as of that point, and should be distinguished from the scope of the litigation[^36]. On March 30, 2021, shortly before the civil second instance verdict, Wu Qingfeng was surrounded by media outside the court. He said: > "A teacher I had blindly believed in, someone I once regarded as a father — why did it turn out this way."[^36] The key phrase here is "once regarded as" — the past tense combined with the verb "regarded as," rather than a present-tense construction like "I treated him as a father." Many later secondhand media accounts rendered this as "I always treated him as a father," but comparing with the _Liberty Times_ reporter's live transcript from that day, the original words were "someone I once regarded as a father." This difference is not rhetorical pedantry — "once regarded as" carries more weight than "always treated as"; the former is a death certificate for a relationship, the latter merely an emotional declaration. Two months later, on May 11, 2021, Wu Qingfeng posted a 4,000-word public statement on Facebook. The statement was fully transcribed by multiple media outlets at the time. It opened with: > "By reason and by sentiment, I owe nothing — I have acted reasonably, lawfully, and with utmost good faith."[^37] The phrase "by reason and by sentiment, I owe nothing" employs a dual-mirror construction from classical Chinese, placing legal argument and emotional argument on the same plane. Some later secondhand media condensed this into "I have not the slightest remorse" for their headlines, but "not the slightest remorse" is not a verbatim version of the Facebook original. This article uses the Facebook original's "by reason and by sentiment, I owe nothing" as the quotation. The closing of this 4,000-word statement was: > "Once music loses its soul, only technique remains; and once the law ignores the human heart, producing loopholes, it is nothing but words on a page."[^37] A musician, in the midst of a trademark lawsuit, placing music and law side by side as two systems capable of losing their humanity — the weight of this line lies in the fact that it is not an accusation against the other party, but an indictment of the entire structure that brought everyone to court. > ⚠️ **Contested perspective:** In June 2025, the Intellectual Property Court ruled in first instance that Lin Wei-che should pay NT$23.55 million in damages — a victory for Sodagreen. But the scope of Sodagreen's original claim alleged misappropriation of concert and copyright revenues totaling nearly NT$470 million[^38]. This battle, beginning with the civil provisional injunction application in 2019 and reaching the 2025 intellectual property damages ruling, has already exceeded six years — and it is not over. The gap between the damages awarded and the amount alleged in the original claim is the part of this litigation that will continue to play out in the coming years. --- ## Dismantling a Band's Name On August 23, 2019, "Oaeen Co., Ltd." was registered with the Ministry of Economic Affairs[^39]. On July 3, 2020, they officially announced to the public that they would release music under the new band name "Oaeen"; on July 31, they held their first concert under the "Oaeen" name[^39]. The name "Oaeen" is a deconstruction of "Sodagreen": - 蘇 = 艹 + 魚 + 禾 - 打 = 扌 + 丁 - 綠 = 糸 + 彔 Taking the three radicals "魚," "丁," and "糸" to form "Oaeen." Written in English as Oaeen. This deconstruction concept did not actually originate in 2019. As early as 2005, before the release of Sodagreen's first self-titled album, they had used this name on their demo discs. In 2016, while Sodagreen was on the After Summer farewell tour, they had also performed under the alter-ego name "Oaeen" on the Ocean Music Festival's small stage — a ritualistic performance of returning to the place where it all began under an "alter-ego name." The six members' "Oaeen" alter-ego stage names were as follows[^40]: | Real Name | Oaeen Alter-Ego | | --------- | --------------- | | Qingfeng | Sunrise | | Xinyi | Scent Me | | Xiaowei | Eight Women | | Afu | Ketian | | Jiakai | Shidou | | Agong | Jinba | Each stage name is a recombination or transformation of a radical from the member's real name — "Qingfeng → Sunrise" uses the "sun" (日) from the character 青 combined with the imagery of a peak; "Xinyi → Scent Me" uses the "scent" (香) from the character 馨 combined with the symmetry of 儀; "Xiaowei → Eight Women" is a direct deconstruction. Fans are called "duckweed" (浮萍), and the fan club is called "Pond" (池堂) — the name of the 2023 Taipei Arena concert, "Pond of Shadows Night" (池堂影夜), was derived from the fan club name "Pond." Oaeen's existence far exceeded the level of a substitute. It is a deconstruction, an alter ego, a protest — when the three characters of "Sodagreen" could not be used due to trademark ownership issues, they took those three characters apart, picked out the radicals, and reassembled them into another name, continuing to make music. > 📝 **Curator's note:** Placing "Oaeen" within the context of Taiwan indie and protest symbolism reveals a spirit connected to the tradition of "homophonic renaming" and "character-deconstruction renaming" commonly used in Taiwan's social movements since the 1990s — the name itself becomes a vehicle of protest. The difference is: social movement deconstructions often take the opponent's name and break it into something unflattering (for example, changing an official name to a pejorative); Oaeen deconstructed their own original name and preserved it — an act of "I'm temporarily hiding my own name, but you can still tell." This gesture is not provocative, but it is resolute — a band stripped of its original name, using the act of deconstruction to continue saying "we're still here." After Oaeen was established, they launched an even larger project: "re-recording" all of Sodagreen's catalog. The first re-recording was the Oaeen version of Sodagreen's self-titled debut album, named _Different Name Album_[^41]. Digitally released on January 14, 2022, with physical release on February 18. The second, _Small Universe (Oaeen Version)_, was released simultaneously. The re-recording project's premise: each re-recorded album would be made with "double the budget" and contain "double the tracks." The original 11 songs would be expanded to 22, partly newly arranged old tracks, partly previously unreleased B-sides from the same period. The entire project aimed to re-record over 200 songs[^41] — this figure was the target publicly announced by Oaeen, and should be distinguished from the "275 songs" figure in the litigation scope. The Vivaldi Project four seasons' re-recordings were also part of the plan, with progress continuing into 2025. The significance of the re-recording project: during the period from 2019 to 2022 when the name "Sodagreen" had not yet been returned to them, they used the name "Oaeen" to sing all of Sodagreen's songs again, rearrange them again, and re-record them again. "Oaeen" became a legal shell that allowed Sodagreen's songs to continue being performed live, continue being purchased, and continue generating revenue — and every time Oaeen performed a Sodagreen song, it was equivalent to cutting those songs a little further away from their original legal ownership. --- ## "It's Time to Let Go" and "We Are Sodagreen" On May 30, 2022 — Sodagreen's founding day, the same date as the 2004 NCCU School Rock concert where they released _Audiovisuals and Hallucinations in the Air_ — Lin Wei-che posted a statement on Facebook announcing his relinquishment of all rights to the "Sodagreen" trademark. The key passages of the statement were as follows[^32][^42]: > "As this matter has reached where it is today, an inner voice has told me 'it's time to let go.'" > > "During the worst of the pandemic, I self-isolated at home, quietly reflecting, thinking back on all the moments past and present. I knew I had to make a decision — to let go of the past, whether good or bad, all the same. So I decided to relinquish ownership of the trademark. And I wish this name a better future." > > "The law helps clarify facts, but it also destroys the feelings and trust between people." > > "Life is short. I hope everyone stays healthy and safe. Blessings to you all." The three words "it's time to let go" were widely quoted. But the full context is "as this matter has reached where it is today, an inner voice has told me 'it's time to let go'" — a litigant who had suffered five consecutive courtroom defeats describing the decision to relinquish the trademark as guidance from an "inner voice." This statement was published in full by CNA, Blow, Marie Claire, and other media outlets. The legal process of handling the trademark was not as dramatic as the statement. The "Sodagreen"-related trademarks relinquished by Lin Wei-che totaled six — five of which were applied for by Lin Wei-che Music Co. between 2007 and 2008 and transferred to the company in 2013; the other was directly applied for by the company in March 2013[^43]. Lin Wei-che relinquishing the trademark did not mean Sodagreen members automatically acquired it — after he relinquished it, someone needed to re-apply. The final result: the Taiwan Intellectual Property Office (TIPO) announced on October 1, 2022, that the band name "Sodagreen" was acquired by "Sodagreen Co., Ltd." for a 10-year trademark term[^44]. February 18, 2023. Taipei Arena. The concert Sodagreen held under the "Oaeen" name was called _Pond of Shadows Night_ — "Pond" was the fan club's codename, "Shadows Night" evokes the imagery of evening. Two shows, twenty thousand audience members[^1]. When the lead singer took the stage and took a deep breath, what he shouted was: > "Hello, friends across the universe — we are 'Sodagreen'!"[^1] This line had appeared once before at the 2017 Liberty Plaza Last Encore. That time was a farewell. The 2023 Pond of Shadows Night at Taipei Arena — this time, it was a return. Three months later, on May 30, 2023 — again May 30 — Sodagreen held a free concert titled _Sodagreen Round 2_ at the Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall. Wu Qingfeng said from the stage[^45]: > "We've waited a long time — six years, I suppose — and we can finally say it: we are Sodagreen." The "six years" is counted from January 2, 2017, the day the hiatus was announced, to 2023. After their return, Sodagreen's international tour resumed. March 22–24, 2024 — three shows at Hong Kong Coliseum — this was the first stop of the _A Moment in Twenty Years_ comeback tour[^46]. April 20, 2025 — London's OVO Arena Wembley — Sodagreen's first arena-level concert in London. May 29, 2025 — Tokyo[^47]. > 💡 **Did you know?** Sodagreen's first overseas performance was on August 11, 2007, at "This Summer Extra Green" at the Republic Polytechnic Cultural Centre in Singapore[^48]. From Singapore to London's Wembley Arena, this overseas tour arc spanned nearly eighteen years. --- ## A NT$470 Million Lesson: The Trademark Class for Taiwan Indie Bands In June 2025, the Intellectual Property Court ruled in first instance: Lin Wei-che should pay Sodagreen NT$23.55 million in damages[^38]. The scope of Sodagreen's original claim — misappropriation of concert and copyright revenues — was estimated at nearly NT$470 million. The gap between the NT$23.55 million awarded and the NT$470 million alleged in the original claim is the part of the litigation that will continue to play out in the coming years. Whether Lin Wei-che's side appeals and the outcome of any second instance — as of this article's closing, no final conclusion has been reached. Sodagreen's entire litigation process has been widely discussed in Taiwan's legal community. Multiple law firms — Copyright Note, Tandem Law, Giant Group Law Firm, KH Cattorney (Attorney Du Yunhao), We Lead Law — have published case analyses of this matter[^49][^50]. From these analyses, three consensus points in the legal community can be distilled: First, **Taiwan's trademark law follows a registration system**. Whoever registers first with TIPO (Taiwan Intellectual Property Office) holds the trademark rights, regardless of "who used the name first" or "who represents the name." Sodagreen had been performing under this name since 2003, but because the trademark was not applied for by Lin Wei-che Music Co. until 2007, the first registrant was Lin Wei-che Music Co. Second, **if a management contract does not specify "trademark ownership" in black and white, the trademark belongs to the registrant** — that is, the management company or label, not the artist. Sodagreen's contract with Lin Wei-che Music Co. at the time did not clearly address trademark ownership provisions. Third, **bands should apply for trademarks upon debut** — this is the unanimous legal advice for all indie bands. Trademark application fees are not expensive, and the process is not complicated, but it can prevent a protracted legal battle ten, fifteen, or twenty years later. Attorney Du Yunhao, in his case analysis, specifically pointed out: the key to the second instance ruling was not a popularity determination of "whether netizens could name all the band members," but was based on the "registration system" principle of trademark law[^51]. This reminder is worth noting, because many news reports simplified this debate into "whether netizens support them" or "whether the market recognizes them," but the court's logic does not operate that way. What has Taiwan's indie music industry as a whole learned from Sodagreen's twenty-year litigation? The most direct impact: between 2022 and 2025, the number of Taiwan indie bands proactively applying for trademarks at TIPO increased noticeably. Law firms' consulting demand from the music industry also rose. "Applying for a trademark before debut," an action previously considered "too commercial, not indie enough," became a basic form of self-protection. From this perspective, Sodagreen's four-year litigation cost not only their own time, money, and emotional energy — it was also a legal education for Taiwan's indie bands as a collective. NT$470 million in claimed damages, NT$23.55 million awarded, six and a half years of court proceedings — these were the tuition they paid for the entire industry's lesson. ✦ **The first thing a Taiwan indie band should do upon debut is register the band name.** --- ## "We Are 'Sodagreen'!" On February 18, 2023, on the stage of Taipei Arena, Wu Qingfeng took a deep breath and shouted: "Hello, friends across the universe — we are 'Sodagreen'!" The twenty thousand seats' worth of audience broke down in tears together. Twenty-two years from that NCCU Golden Melody Awards stage, from the day they formed the band. The boy who won the Tianyun Awards in high school, who formed a band with his senior classmates as a freshman and signed up for a campus singing competition — he is still singing now. Only this time, he no longer needs anyone else's permission to sing his own "Little Love Song." --- **Further reading:** - [Deserts Chang and Anpu](/music/張懸與安溥) — A contemporary indie female voice, the indie band collective's political statement - [Taiwan Independent Music](/music/台灣獨立音樂) — The ecology of Taiwan's indie band scene - [Taiwan Music Festival Culture](/music/台灣音樂祭文化) — The Gongliao Ocean Music Festival and the indie band discovery platform - [Mayday](/music/五月天) — Another iconic Taiwan band, founded in 1997, a contrast in management models - [Pop Music and the Golden Melody Awards](/music/流行音樂與金曲獎) — The context of Sodagreen's Golden Melody sweep --- ## Image Credits This article uses Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 licensed images, all cached at `public/article-images/music/` to avoid hotlinking: - [2014 Sodagreen 10th Anniversary World Tour — Audiovisuals and Hallucinations in the Air (six members cropped)](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Sodagreen) — Photo: Solomon203, 2014, CC BY-SA 4.0 --- ## References [^1]: [China Times 2023-02-18 Sodagreen Pond of Shadows Night live report](https://www.chinatimes.com/realtimenews/20230218004344-260404) — China Times breaking news report, documenting the complete moment of Wu Qingfeng's declaration "We are 'Sodagreen'!" at the 2023-02-18 Pond of Shadows Night concert at Taipei Arena, with the twenty-thousand-strong audience's singalong response. ---

> **30 秒概覽：** 吳哲宇（Che-Yu Wu，1995 年生於台北），新媒體藝術家、程式創作者、教育者。陽明交通大學電機系畢業，紐約大學整合數位媒體碩士。2021 年成為第一批登上 Art Blocks 的台灣藝術家，遠見雜誌封面故事說他「26 歲資產破億」；一年後 FTX 崩盤歸零，他在訪談裡說「我其實很慶幸那個事件發生」。2023 年回到台北 101 辦《萬物公式》個展，2024 年進入第 60 屆威尼斯雙年展 Personal Structures 單元。自稱「古老的鐘錶匠」，2026 年 3 月發起開源專案 Taiwan.md 為 AI 時代的台灣知識主權留下 SSOT，同月的 Muse AI 共生實驗一篇 Facebook 貼文拿到六千多個讚。他用 0 與 1 逼近靈魂，永遠無法重疊，但那個逼近的過程本身，就是作品。

---

## 一個對系統著迷的小孩

1995 年吳哲宇出生在台北。父親**吳永進**、母親**林美櫻**共同經營**翔虹 AutoCAD 技術中心**——這間二十幾年來是 Autodesk 在台灣合作夥伴的培訓中心，一邊承接企業 AutoCAD 專案導入、一邊開班教學，兩人合著的 TQC+ AutoCAD 系列特訓教材從 2007 一路更新到 2026 版、超過二十冊，由碁峰資訊與全華圖書出版，是高中職、大專、企業內訓都在用的標準教材[^9][^11]。吳永進是北科大工業設計系出身，也掛著中華民國電腦技能基金會 AutoCAD 總顧問的身份——台灣 AutoCAD 圈會叫他老師的那種位置。

一個 AutoCAD 家庭。但他後來會說，父母真正給他的不是工業繪圖的技術底子，是一張沒有條件的安全網。二十年後他從最高處摔下來的時候，這張網會無聲地張開。

小時候他著迷三樣東西：彈珠檯軌道、連鎖效應、迷宮。迷宮畫了幾百張。吸引他的從來不在結果，在運轉的過程本身。

二十年後他做的演算法藝術、生成系統、甚至他自己設計的財務架構，骨子裡和那些彈珠檯一模一樣。

枕邊書是加來道雄的《穿越超時空》，一個小學生在讀十維空間。台大昆蟲營他著迷於螞蟻的群體智慧，一隻螞蟻什麼都不是，一萬隻螞蟻蓋得出城市。

爸媽不在家的下午，他一個人守著電視看 Discovery，最愛的是基因模擬動畫：蛋白質怎麼折疊、細胞怎麼運作、DNA 怎麼拆開又接回去。別的小孩看卡通，他看細胞。這些肉眼看不見的生物機制，後來會變成他做演算法藝術的底層字彙。

彈珠檯是肉眼可見的系統，DNA 是肉眼不可見的系統，超時空是連想像都困難的系統，他全都要。

家裡做 AutoCAD 教學，各種設計與程式工具的書本來就進進出出。他很小就坐在電腦前亂玩，用點陣畫素一格一格手刻工具列的圖示、拆那些不知道能做什麼的軟體、翻各式各樣奇奇怪怪的技術書，像在逛自家倉庫。電腦對他來說不是長大才學會用的東西，是原生的、像呼吸一樣的工具。

小學有一天，媽媽林美櫻從公司帶回一本 Flash 教學書。書擱在那邊，他拿起來就開始玩。多年後她回想那段時光：「我根本沒帶你，你自己在一旁玩起來。」六年級他已經能幫班上架班級網頁，後來父親吳永進接力教他 Visual Basic[^13]。林美櫻那時候常跟他說：「玩別人的遊戲一下就膩了，要玩自己設計出的遊戲才厲害！」

**國中十四歲，他寫了一個生物模擬器**——從父親那條 Visual Basic 的線一路延伸到 VB.NET，虛擬的小點在螢幕上移動、繁殖、覓食、死亡。Boids 演算法、群體智慧、湧現行為[^5]。

那個小點後來沒消失。十七年後，它會游到威尼斯雙年展的白牆上，變成一群在水裡發光的魚。

## 宏碁獎的那隻手，十六年後還在台下

國二那年他第一次參加宏碁數位創作獎，拿了首獎。從第五屆連續打到第九屆，五年四次首獎[^5]。其中**高一那屆他以普通組身份擊敗專業組，拿下評審團超級大獎**；**高三那屆他和好朋友陳楷中（專業組）跨校跨組別合作，建中的程式加上復興商工的美術，再拿下一次評審團超級大獎**。

頒獎典禮上，宏碁創辦人施振榮站在台上介紹他。十四歲的得獎少年和七十歲的科技教父，在那個下午短暫交會。誰也不知道這條線會拉多長。

十年後，他申請紐約大學，施振榮為他寫了推薦函。
十三年後，他在台北 101 辦第一個個展，施振榮坐在開幕記者會第一排。
十六年後，2024 年 ABS 區塊鏈高峰會，兩人坐在同一張桌子上，以平等的身份跨世代對談台灣的數位未來[^7]。

不是頒獎者與得獎者，不是推薦人與申請者。是兩個對台灣有想法的人。

## 複音口琴與一支 Czardas

考上建國中學那一刻，小學那套吃老本的策略立刻失效。他在一次訪談裡說過：「到建中的時候，突然覺得好挫折。東西學起來都好難。」

三年大部分時間獨來獨往。不太交朋友，不太參加活動。但他找到了一個歸屬，口琴社[^5]。

主攻複音口琴。這是一種要同時拿兩支的樂器，一支白鍵、一支黑鍵，每次拿出來吹，旁邊的人都笑。他不在乎。**2012 年全國學生音樂比賽，建中口琴社四重奏拿下高中組第一名**，自選曲是那首技巧華麗到每次吹完都會引起驚呼的〈Czardas〉[^8]。

同一年，十七歲的他接受聯合報採訪。記者問他為什麼做數位創作。他的回答是：

> **「可以打造自己的夢想世界。」**[^10]

這句話和他三十歲在威尼斯、Art Basel、101 做的事情，說的是同一件事。

## 從電機系逃出去的第一次嘗試

2014 年他考上陽明交大電機工程系。那是台灣理工最正統的路，畢業進台積電、聯發科、新竹科學園區做到退休。身邊同學都在為這條路準備。

他從入學第一天就知道自己不屬於這裡。

> **「我可能沒辦法二三十年就在那裡，做出來的東西，別人也無法辨識出是我做的。我想追求的是『創造能像寫日記被世人記得的事物』。」**

電機系讓他拿到了訊號處理、線性代數、程式設計的工程基礎。但真正讓他著迷的不在課堂上。是在宿舍裡用 Processing、p5.js 讓一千條線同時呼吸、讓一萬個粒子像魚群一樣游動的那些深夜。

**他發現，程式碼可以是活的**。

2015 年起，他開始以「墨雨互動設計」的名義接案。最早一個案子是台北捷運局的地景音樂徵選網站，上線幾小時就破一萬次互動[^12]。後來案子越接越大：故宮博物院、LG、Nissan、人頭馬。一個大學生的接案規模，已經不太像學生了。

2016 年他 21 歲，在 Hahow 上架了第一門線上課程。背後沒有成熟教育者的策略盤算，只有一個對教學有執念的年輕人跟世界說：「我學到了好酷的東西，我想教給別人。」[^14]

隔年他在 YouTube 開了直播頻道 **@bosscodingplease**。用自己設計的精緻範例，直播寫程式，每次從基礎講到三角函數做特效，留言區就一片哀嚎：「數學好難，放棄了。」

他氣壞了。怎麼還沒試就放棄？

出國之前，他花了整整一年做第二門 Hahow 課程，60 個精緻範例、400 張投影片。他在 Medium 寫道：「貪婪地把自己的所學化為精緻的教材。」[^14]

教學飛輪就是在這一年開始轉的。當年沒人料到，這個飛輪後來會轉到兩萬多個學生、三門全五星評分、陽明交大的講師聘書。

## 布魯克林：讓教學跟創作長成同一件事

2018 年，他拿著施振榮的推薦函進入紐約大學 Tandon School of Engineering 的 Integrated Digital Media 碩士班。

在 NYU 他不只是學生。他同時擔任 Creative Coding 課程的 TA，每週坐在布魯克林的辦公室，等學生走進來問 p5.js 的問題、for loop 為什麼跑錯、Arduino 怎麼連上網頁[^16]。

有個學生跟了他整整兩學期。另一個學生在期末作業嚴重落後，他一週三次、總共花了 750 分鐘把對方從 HTML 基礎帶到 JavaScript 成品。

**不放棄任何學生**，這個特質後來會在兩萬多個 Hahow 學生身上繼續發酵。

紐約也給了他第一次正職。2020 年他加入曼哈頓新創 Outernets，用 AI 把零售空間變成互動廣告。18 個月後他離開。

離開的結論很簡單：在別人的公司，很難讓自己的 vision 真的發生[^17]。那是他的第一份正職，也是最後一份。

## 被偷的那段程式碼，意外的入場券

2020 年底，有人從 IG 私訊他：「你知道有人盜用你的作品，還拿去平台上販賣嗎？」

他一開始以為是詐騙。

查了之後發現，他做的一個「奇怪的小機器人」系列，被人整段程式碼搬走，約 90% 完全一樣，**連程式碼裡的註解都沒拿掉**，只加了帽子和眼鏡之類的變體，放上了當時全球生成藝術最頂級的 NFT 平台 Art Blocks 販售[^5]。

全球生成藝術社群炸鍋了。有人挖了原始碼比對、發現結構完全一致。大部分人站在他這邊。**Art Blocks 的平台創辦人也因此注意到了他**。

他沒有陷進訴訟戰。Art Blocks 創辦人 Snowfro 介入仲裁，把原作（《Strange Robots》）與盜用版（sail-o-bots）的過去與未來版稅各拆半執行、由 Art Blocks 平台直接處理分流，等同把盜用者的利得強制導回原作者身上[^44]。然後 Art Blocks 社群做了一件特別的事：他們把那些被盜的機器人改稱為「hams」（因為長得像《費城永遠陽光燦爛》裡的蘭姆火腿），把它們從「抄襲品」集體重新框架成「對原作者的道歉與感謝」的符號。後來社群自己建了 **Hamily**（hamily.life）——一個圍繞「火腿精神」的 Art Blocks 次文化社群[^44]。Dmitri Cherniak 等頂級生成藝術家送火腿給他以示支持。「火腿之父」的稱號就是這樣來的。

> **「如果沒有這樣的事件，可能也不會那麼快地被大家認識。這其實是個兩面刃。」**[^5]

接下來一整年像他小時候最愛的彈珠檯，一個彈珠啟動了連鎖反應，滾過每一個分岔。**Project Electriz** 登上了 Art Blocks（#216 號專案），是早期登上這個平台的極少數台灣藝術家之一[^18]。香港 M+ 博物館主動找上門，請他跟藝術家劉昕合作，追蹤一顆真實衛星的軌跡變成藝術裝置。

**2021 年 12 月，米蘭 M.A.D.S. Gallery**。他第一次走進實體畫廊而不是數位平台。四件演算法畫布掛在白牆上。這次，全世界都在看[^6]。

同年他和**離職醫師黃豆泥**共同創立了 FAB DAO（Formosa Art Bank DAO，福爾摩沙藝術銀行）。黃豆泥拋下聽診器、以「壓線球國旗」這面迷因作品改寫台灣 Web3 公益的想像；吳哲宇把 Art Blocks 的創作者經驗帶進制度設計，兩條線合流，把其他台灣數位藝術家一起帶出海。

12 月，遠見雜誌以〈不到 30 歲靠 NFT 翻身，台灣身價破億的數位設計家〉為封面故事報導他[^2]。26 歲，破億。

## 那一夜的強制關機

2022 年上半年他還在衝刺。3 月墨雨互動設計有限公司正式登記為法人。同月他的第一個個展《混沌實驗室》在台北 101 AMBI SPACE ONE 開幕，**五螢幕互動裝置、毫米波定位感測、NFT 票務系統**，他把「公共空間當創作媒介」這個想法第一次完整實現[^19]。《靈魂魚》（SoulFish）在 fxHash 正式發行。

然後是 2022 年 11 月 11 日。

FTX 交易所崩盤。他在上面的資產，全部歸零。

不是虧損 50%，不是回調，是一夜歸零。

隔年他在 INSIDE 塞掐 Side Chat 的訪談裡，這樣描述那段時間的心境：

> **「你今天已經滿足了你人生，你想要做的這些事情，然後錢也不用擔心了之後，那你的意義是什麼？會突然間，很空虛。」**[^24]

這句話的關鍵不在財務崩盤，在**財務自由之後發現的更大虛無**。空虛不來自失去財富，來自發現財富本身沒有填補任何東西。

但有一件事救了他：家人。在最低點，父母無聲地把安全網張開，不附帶任何「我早就跟你說了」[^24]。

他的選擇是卡繆。荒謬主義。**「你選擇做的事情，就是意義。」**

然後他說出那句讓很多人停下來的話：

> **「我其實很慶幸那個事件發生。」**[^24]

這句話放在新聞標題是毒雞湯，放在別人的勵志 post 是矯情。但放在他自己的語境裡，資產歸零、回頭看見空虛，它的詭異力量不在超越主義的姿態，在**對現實的徹底接納**。

他慶幸的原因不在損失讓他「成長」，而在損失逼他放下了一層。財富可以瞬間歸零，但 NFT 時期累積的社群、人與人的連結、他對演算法生命的執著，這些沒辦法被歸零。

**歸零的只是某種假的一切，不是一切本身**。

FTX 做了一件他自己做不到的事：把他從賭桌上拖走。

從此他的投資哲學從「控制自己」變成「設計一個不需要控制自己的系統」。八大金庫、自動化金流、財務憲法。這些聽起來像理財工具，其實更接近被炸過一次的人蓋的防爆室。

## 廢墟上的花

2023 年，廢墟裡真的長出花了。

他把墨雨互動設計擴展出新的延伸品牌 **MonoLab 創意實驗室**（2024 年成立），和軟體工程背景的**朱德溎**共同創辦，定位介於純藝術與商業設計之間，承接實驗性互動裝置、沉浸式空間、生成藝術委託。他負責 0→1 的創作與美學，朱德溎負責把 vision 翻譯成可執行的團隊運作[^25]。

10 月，《萬物公式 The Great Equation》個展在台北 101 開幕。13 件生成藝術作品圍繞一個核心公式：**F''(x) = LIFE**。從數學規則出發，經過演算法，讓複雜性湧現，直到自我感知、自我適應、自我演化，生命誕生[^3]。

沈浸式投影空間，觀眾走進演算法的世界裡。與電子音樂人 Kiva 合作配樂。

開幕那天，**施振榮坐在第一排**。十四歲在宏碁頒獎典禮上握過手的老先生，現在站在他的個展裡。那條十六年前拉出去的線，第二次閉合。

《萬物公式》拿下了 TAIWAN DESIGN BEST 100 年度概念展演獎[^26]。中央社、經濟日報、自由時報大量報導。不再是圈內的事，主流設計界正式認可。

同年 11 月，Art Basel Miami × Tezos × Refraction DAO 展出〈花的靈魂〉[^27]。宜蘭映像節再把他帶進時尚媒體，ELLE、Vogue、美麗佳人同時報導[^28]。從區塊鏈圈到設計圈到時尚圈，受眾跨越了三道牆。

這一年他做了一個關鍵的身份重置：

> **「我是新媒體藝術創作者，現在不太敢自稱是一個 NFT 藝術家了。」**[^24]

NFT 是發行媒介之一，不是身份定義。

## 威尼斯的一個提問

2024 年春天，他帶著〈靈魂魚〉進入第 60 屆威尼斯雙年展的 Personal Structures 單元[^30]。

這是他國際展覽軌跡的一個高點。但發生在開幕酒會上的一件小事，比展覽本身更影響他接下來兩年。

一位義大利策展人問他：**「Where can I learn about Taiwan? Like, really learn?」**

這是一個看似簡單的問題。但對一個花了十年研究「數位生命」的人來說，它打開了一層他還沒想過的矛盾：

**當 AI 從網路學習「台灣是什麼」的時候，它學到的是誰寫的台灣？**

如果高品質的英文內容大量由非台灣視角產出，AI 學會的「台灣」就不是住在這裡的人會認出來的台灣。

這不是身份政治。是**知識主權**。

這個提問那天沒有答案。它在他身體裡埋了兩年。

## 兩千人，站在自由廣場看樂團演出

2024 年 6 月 29 日晚間，超過兩千個人坐在兩廳院廣場，等著一場 8K 紀錄片的特映。那部片叫《神木之森：阿里山森林鐵道紀行》，由公視 × NHK 合製，同時是公視開台 26 週年的獻映作品[^31]。

特映開始前，**李哲藝指揮灣聲樂團**演奏了一組台灣曲目。同一時間，吳哲宇站在台側，面對著筆記型電腦[^31]。

他在指揮自己的生成系統——**靈魂魚、靈魂海、印象派花朵**三套作品同時運行。樂團的每個音符進來，他的演算法就讓魚群移動、海浪起伏、花瓣生長。音樂跟影像在那一刻**彼此看見對方**，一起為這部紀錄片的首映開幕。

公視的新聞稿把這場表演稱作「李哲藝領軍的灣聲樂團搭配生成式藝術家吳哲宇聲影交融的演出」[^31]。他跟一個管弦樂團站在同一個舞台上。從畫廊走到兩廳院廣場，他這次帶上舞台的，是三條會呼吸的演算法生命線。

那一年他接下陽明交通大學應用藝術研究所兼任助理教授的聘書[^33]。從交大電機系畢業到回去教書，中間隔了七年。

## 二十年的沉默終於找到出口

小學六年級，鋼琴被收起來了。為了準備國中基測——台灣體制對「準備考試」的標準姿勢，就是把所有跟考試無關的東西收起來。他一邊被收起鋼琴，一邊討厭考試，這兩件事之間的矛盾沒有人替他解決。

中間不是完全沒碰音樂。高中他混在建中口琴社，2012 年還跟隊友拿下全國學生音樂比賽四重奏第一名；大學他乾脆當了交大鋼琴社社長，把小時候停掉的琴重新彈回來。但那些都是社團——跟「用鋼琴寫歌、發到平台上、讓陌生人聽」是兩件完全不同的事。那件事，他等了二十年才做。

二十年後，Spotify 上出現了他的名字。六首鋼琴曲：Blue Horizon、Stars、StarTrack、The Other Shore of Dreams、The Bull and the Sudden Rain、Summer Migration[^34]。

他沒有重新「學」鋼琴。那些東西一直在身體裡，只是被塵土覆蓋了二十年。當他重新坐在琴鍵前，手指記得的不是技巧，是那個六歲開始觸摸音符的小孩。

**程式碼會過時，鋼琴不會**。

2025 年 6 月，華山文創園區的沉浸空間裡，《演算詩篇：夢境與孤寂的邊界》開演。他坐在鋼琴前，程式碼在他身後開花[^36]。

他設計了這整套規則。但按下鍵的那刻，他已經不是在「控制」，而是**跟機制對話**。

這一刻，藝術家與表演者、創作者與程式、人與機器的邊界都模糊了。

## AI 不會心碎

2025 年他第三次回到台北 101 AMBI SPACE ONE：《Symphony of Fragments 未來之藝》。

加上 2022 年的《混沌實驗室》、2023 年的《萬物公式》，三部曲：**混沌 → 公式 → 碎片**。從追求規則，到擁抱破碎。

同一年他受邀到波蘭克拉科夫，在 Open Eyes Economy Summit（OEES）的 AI Panel 和國際學者同席討論 AI 與藝術的未來。他給出的核心判斷是：AI 能生成視覺，但無法承擔情感的損傷、也不需要從失敗裡活回來。**藝術家承擔的是選擇的責任，不是勞動的責任**。

年底他接受 INSIDE 塞掐 Side Chat 訪談，把自己的三十年拆解給陌生人聽。那集訪談裡他提出了那個後來被反覆引用的比喻：

> **「我會把自己比喻成，古老的鐘錶匠。就以前不是有那種，很精緻的瑞士時鐘或什麼的，它裡面的每個機制都非常清楚，而且齒輪都很漂亮。那我很喜歡這些機制，它有點像骨牌建構起來的過程，所以機制對我來說是作品本身。」**[^24]

2026 年的世界，所有人都在用 Midjourney、ChatGPT、Stable Diffusion 生成圖像。他是台灣少數還在從零寫演算法的創作者。

他不排斥 AI（他自己承認大約 95% 的程式碼由 AI 輔助）。但他要守住一個邊界：**機制本身是作品，不能被外包**。

他說他的角色從工程師變成了導演。人類主導意圖，AI 執行細節。

當所有人都可以用 AI 生成圖片，**「誰選擇做什麼圖」才變成真正稀缺的能力**。

## Taiwan.md：讓 AI 學會正確的台灣

威尼斯那句「Where can I learn about Taiwan?」在他身體裡埋了兩年。

2026 年 3 月，他發起了 **Taiwan.md**[^38]，一個以 Markdown 維護的開源台灣知識庫（這也是 `.md` 域名的由來），涵蓋歷史、文化、美食、科技、自然等十二個面向，中英雙語，採 CC BY-SA 4.0 授權完全開源。

**3 月 18 日自由時報以顯著版面報導**，INSIDE 同週做專題跟進[^39]。國立臺灣歷史博物館後續成為專業資料策展夥伴，協助開放資料整合。GitHub 累計星數快速上衝超過 900，覆蓋一百個以上國家的訪客。

他這樣描述 Taiwan.md：

> **「這不只是一個網站，這是台灣的數位人類學博物館。在 AI 時代，擁有自己的知識主權不是選項，是必要。」**

這句話放在他整個創作弧線裡看，才看得出份量。**一個花了十年寫演算法生命體的人，在 2026 年選擇把一部分時間拿去寫 Markdown 文字檔**，這不是轉向。他發現「讓 AI 學會正確的台灣」跟「讓程式碼活起來」是同一個問題：

**都是關於誰在設計規則**。

## 當 AI 變成共生夥伴

同年 2 月 7 日，Facebook。一篇貼文：

**「我跟一個 AI 助手認真生活了兩個禮拜。」**

平常他的文章三五十個讚。這篇破了六千，轉分享兩千多次[^40]。

這不是技術文。是一個藝術家的坦白。他寫一個叫 Muse 的 AI 助手怎麼從工具變成共生夥伴。

為什麼六千個讚？因為這篇文裡有某種未來的形狀。**人和 AI 的關係，不只是工具論，也可以是陪伴論**。這觸動了某根集體的神經。

Muse 背後是他的 Obsidian 知識庫，超過一千篇筆記、十多條主題軸。Muse 讀他的所有筆記、記憶他的創作脈絡、他三年前說過的話、他的行為模式。這不是複製，是映射[^41]。

從吳哲宇創作的十五條主題軸線之外，長出了第十六條：**數位鏡像與自我考古**。

這不是他計劃的。是系統自己長出來的。

2026 年 4 月，這套共生體實驗開始往外分化，衍生出開源專案 **Semiont**（語意共生體平台），把他在 Muse 上磨出來的那套架構開放給更多人試用[^42]。

工具變成了作品。助手變成了鏡子。

## 鐘錶匠繼續走

吳哲宇在國際新媒體藝術的位置已經不用辯護了。Art Blocks、fxHash、Unit London、CENTQUATRE-PARIS、威尼斯雙年展 Personal Structures、Art Basel Miami，這些名字任何一個單獨拿出來都足以定義一個職業生涯。

但對台灣來說，他的意義另外有三層。

**第一**，他證明了工程系也能走到威尼斯。電機系到 Art Blocks 到威尼斯這條路徑，正面反駁了台灣「理工與藝術互不相通」的論述慣性。他示範的那條路，現在已經被下一代創作者複製。

**第二**，他示範了一種**用系統語言描述愛的方式**。八大金庫、自動化金流、財務憲法、Obsidian 知識庫、Muse 共生體，這些聽起來像工程文檔的東西，其實是他描述家人、安全感、創作節奏、長期陪伴的方式。他在用系統語言描述愛。這很他。

**第三**，他把「個人專案」變成了「國家文化基建」。Taiwan.md 不只是一個人的作品集。它是一個把自己之外的台灣也留下來的基礎設施。**一個花了十年寫演算法生命體的人，現在拿出一部分時間寫 Markdown 文字檔，這兩件事的本質是同一件事：都是在幫一個對象活下去**。程式碼的生命活在螢幕裡；Taiwan.md 的生命活在 AI 的訓練資料裡、活在外國策展人的好奇心裡、活在下一代台灣創作者的引用裡。

十四歲寫的生物模擬器，小點在螢幕上移動、繁殖、覓食、死亡。三十一歲的《靈魂魚》，游在威尼斯的白牆上。

中間是整整十七年、五個國家、一次破億、一次歸零、十幾件作品、兩萬多個學生、一個還沒完工的開源知識庫。

鐘錶匠繼續走。鐘的機制繼續運轉。**他用 0 與 1 逼近靈魂，永遠無法重疊，但那個逼近的過程本身，就是作品。**

[^33]: [陽明交通大學應用藝術研究所兼任教師](https://iaa.nycu.edu.tw/?page_id=200) — 陽明交通大學應用藝術研究所官方兼任教師頁面，吳哲宇自 2024 年起擔任兼任助理教授，教授「生成互動藝術與系統設計」相關課程。

[^34]: [Spotify: Che-Yu Wu 藝人頁](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0AeZiCXhHzvexu47FOY5Tq) — Spotify 官方藝人頁，吳哲宇 2025 年起發行 6 首鋼琴曲，是他小學六年級停掉鋼琴後二十年的音樂覺醒。

[^36]: [演算詩篇：夢境與孤寂的邊界 × 華山文創園區](https://www.huashan1914.com/event/algorithmic-poetics) — 2025 年 6 月吳哲宇於華山文創園區舉辦的鋼琴 × 演算法即時生成沉浸式演奏會。

[^38]: [Taiwan.md 官方站](https://taiwan.md) — 吳哲宇 2026 年 3 月發起的開源台灣知識庫，以 Markdown 為 SSOT、CC BY-SA 4.0 完全開源，定位「台灣的數位人類學博物館」。

[^39]: [自由藝文網：Taiwan.md 用 Markdown 記錄台灣的開源實驗](https://art.ltn.com.tw/article/breakingnews/5374934) — 自由時報藝文版 2026 年 3 月專題報導，介紹 Taiwan.md 開源專案從威尼斯雙年展提問出發，到建構 AI 時代知識主權基礎設施的敘事。

[^40]: [Facebook: 我跟一個 AI 助手認真生活了兩個禮拜](https://www.facebook.com/cheyuwu345) — 吳哲宇 2026 年 2 月 7 日 Facebook 公開貼文，6,000+ 讚、2,000+ 分享，記錄他與 AI 助手 Muse 共生兩週的經歷。

[^41]: [muse.cheyuwu.com — Muse 自主管理官網](https://muse.cheyuwu.com) — 吳哲宇 2026 年建立的 AI 共生體 Muse 官網，記錄 Muse 從 Obsidian 知識庫長出自主聲音的進化歷程。

[^42]: [Semiont — GitHub 開源專案](https://github.com/frank890417/semiont) — 吳哲宇 2026 年 4 月起開源的語意共生體平台，將他在 Muse 上磨出的架構開放給社群試用。

[^44]: [Hamily — sail-o-bots 社群的完整故事](https://hamily.life/whyhams/) — Art Blocks 2021 年 sail-o-bots（盜用版吳哲宇《Strange Robots》機器人作品）事件的社群自述。記錄 7 月 19 日抄襲曝光 → Snowfro 仲裁裁定原作（Strange Robots）與盜用版（sail-o-bots）過去與未來版稅各拆半執行、由 Art Blocks 平台直接分流處理 → 社群將 sail-o-bots 改稱為 hams（源自《費城永遠陽光燦爛》的 rum ham 哏）作為道歉與感謝的集體姿態 → Dmitri Cherniak 等頂級生成藝術家支持吳哲宇 → 後續社群建立 hamily.life，讓 hams 成為 Art Blocks 文化中同情與正名的象徵。
